Ships first as (a) the escape hatch for unsupported overlap classes, (b) the reference implementation for differential testing, (c) the primary fuzz target. Deterministic tie-breaks throughout.

STAGE 2 (progressive replacement): the curve-aware pipeline — fat-line-pruned quad–quad intersection with Newton polish, winding-number classification, and curve stitching — replacing the fallback class-by-class. Numerical posture: double-double predicates where needed, WITHOUT exactness overclaims; a WRITTEN topology spec per degenerate class (tangencies, shared endpoints, overlapping segments, zero-area lobes) before code handles it.

ACCEPTANCE IS TOPOLOGY-AWARE, not point-wise: point-in-fill sampling grids, winding-number equivalence, component/hole counts, Euler characteristic, boolean algebraic identities (A∪B = B∪A, A∖B ∩ B = ∅, ...), and multi-scale raster equivalence between stage-1 and stage-2 results; plus captured skia-pathops fixtures (generated once from the Reference toolchain) and adversarial fuzzing with resource budgets (pathological intersection counts are a DoS surface — §16.5).

Risk R4 rule: unsupported overlap classes route PERMANENTLY to the fallback rather than shipping wrong topology. Union/Difference/Intersection/Exclusion mobjects (W7) consume this.

Acceptance: stage 1 green under the full acceptance battery + fuzzing; stage-2 classes each land with their topology spec + differential tests vs stage 1.

title: W2: space_ops + Rotation conventions — scipy semantics fixed, tested at singularities
description:
Implement space_ops signature-for-signature and the quaternion/Euler conventions module (§7.5, §2.2). The Reference's space_ops follows scipy Rotation's conventions — quaternion sign, composition order, as_euler("zxz") extraction, gimbal behavior — and users' camera code assumes them. We fix these conventions AS DOCUMENTED SEMANTICS (they are meaning, not implementation detail), implemented over fnp-linalg / fsci, tested explicitly at the hard points: gimbal-lock configurations, quaternion double-cover sign choices, near-identity and near-pi rotations, axis-angle degeneracies.

Scope: rotation_matrix, quaternions (mult, conjugate, from/to axis-angle), Euler conversions with the "zxz" behavior pinned, angle_between_vectors, normalize, cross/dot conveniences, z_to_vector, rotation_about_z fast paths, get_unit_normal (Appendix B: kept), 2D↔3D point promotion rules, complex↔R2 helpers — the full call-site inventory from the Reference's space_ops module, verified against Appendix A consumers.

Determinism note: everything here is f64 semantic math (§6.1); transcendentals route through the fmn-dmath seam so certified renders get bit-stable Euler/axis conversions (CameraFrame animations are transcendental-dense).

Acceptance: convention fixtures generated from scipy on the pinned Reference environment (loose f32 tolerance per §16.4); singularity tests with DEFINED outputs (no NaN escapes); property tests (composition associativity within tolerance, double-cover q ≡ -q, round-trip Euler→quat→Euler within branch conventions); doc page stating the conventions normatively.

title: W2: The SVG document processor — user SVGs, hardened, with explicit accept/reject
description:
Implement SVGMobject's substrate as a real document processor (§7.6; displaces svgelements). Scope is USER SVGs — with dvisvgm gone, there are no TeX-output quirks to replicate; this is a clean, hardened importer.

Feature surface: XML tokenization with namespaces and entity handling; viewBox / preserveAspectRatio / unit resolution; nested transforms; defs/use with expansion limits and cycle detection; presentation-attribute and style-attribute cascade (the subset SVGs in the wild actually use — document what's out); group opacity; fill-rule (nonzero/evenodd); stroke cap/join/dash; path data parsing (all commands, arcs converted via the endpoint→center parameterization then to quads through W2's converter); basic shapes (rect/circle/ellipse/line/polyline/polygon); EXPLICIT accept-or-reject decisions for clips, masks, gradients, patterns — each either implemented, or rejected with a precise named error (never silently dropped).

Security posture (§16.5, R14 — this parses untrusted input): no external entities, no remote references of any kind, bounded document size / nesting depth / path-command counts, finite-number validation on every coordinate, decompression limits if compressed input is ever accepted. Fuzz target from day one with resource-budget assertions (must error, never hang).

Acceptance: a fixture corpus of real-world SVGs with golden path/style output; the accept/reject matrix documented; adversarial fixtures (entity bombs, deep nesting, huge counts) rejected within budget; fuzzer wired into W10's campaign.

title: W2: Scalar-field isolines + ear-clip triangulation utilities
description:
Two owned geometry utilities (§7.7–7.8):

(1) ISOLINES: an adaptive quadtree isoline extractor for ImplicitFunction (displaces the isosurfaces package), honoring the Reference's min_depth / max_quads API knobs as BUDGET semantics (the knobs bound work; they do not promise a particular mesh). Everything underdefined in the original is DEFINED here: traversal order (deterministic), truncation behavior when max_quads is hit (which regions coarsen, in what order), segment orientation convention, domain-boundary handling, and NaN/undefined-region behavior (functions may divide by zero — cells containing non-finite samples are handled by a stated rule, not UB). Quality target: "ours, and good" — adaptive refinement should visibly beat uniform sampling on curvature-varying implicit curves.

(2) EAR-CLIP: a polygon triangulator with hole support (displaces mapbox-earcut) — deterministic tie-breaks, honest acceptance criteria (valid triangulation of valid input; no Delaunay quality pretensions). It is a UTILITY for booleans' flatten fallback, mesh export, and future mesh work; the live fill path needs no triangulation at all (§10.2 rasterizes winding coverage analytically — that independence is a design feature, keep it).

Acceptance: isoline determinism tests (same field + budget ⇒ identical segments); budget-semantics tests (max_quads respected exactly); NaN-region tests; known-curve accuracy tests (circle/lemniscate implicit forms vs analytic); triangulator validity property tests (area preservation, no flipped/degenerate triangles, hole containment) over fuzzed polygons.

title: W3: The Stage arena — generational handles, rooted lifetimes, CoW snapshots (G0-1 ratified)
description:
Implement Marionette's ownership core exactly as ratified by G0-1 (§8.1, D-11). The design under ratification: a Stage arena owning all mobject storage; generational Mob handles (index + generation, invalidating on free); rooted lifetime semantics (what keeps an object alive: scene membership, parent links, explicit roots held by user code); copy-on-write snapshots backing SceneState, animation begin-states (§9.5's frame-parallel purity depends on cheap begin-state snapshots), Studio undo, and replay barriers.

Required behaviors (the ten G0-1 scenarios become this bead's permanent regression suite): detached construction; cross-group composition; multiple parents with cached family flattening (submobjects / parents / family with dirty invalidation — §1.1 API surface); removal with live handles outstanding (handles observe a defined state, never UB); the two-scene policy (defined: an object's relationship to multiple scenes is whatever G0-1 ratified); copy; Python proxy identity across collection; updater closures holding handles; snapshot/restore.

The public fluent Rust API is what the G0-1 prototype PROVED — implement that shape, not a new invention (current lean: scoped stage context + deferred-command .animate recording, §15.1).

Interlocks: the RecordBuffer bead layers per-object data storage into this arena; the family/positional bead consumes the family cache; fmn-python's proxy layer (W10) maps PyObjects onto handles.

Acceptance: the ten-scenario suite green as permanent tests; CoW snapshot cost measured (O(touched), not O(scene)); generational-safety tests (stale handle access is a defined error); miri-clean under the workspace's no-unsafe posture (trivially, but assert it).
notes: In progress 2026-07-22: production arena landed in crates/fmn-mobject (stage.rs arena + family cache w/ dirty invalidation, record.rs = ratified V1-V6 view-protocol core seeding fm-cus, mobject.rs detached values). 14 tests green: ten G0-1 scenarios (permanent suite) + generational safety + O(touched) snapshot cost + family-cache invalidation + cycle refusal (typed error; Reference recurses forever on cyclic families — Behavior Note to finalize with fm-jru). Updater slot minimal by design (insertion order + call_now-once); full semantics are fm-yra. Miri run pending.
close_reason: Production Stage arena landed in fmn-mobject exactly as G0-1 ratified: generational stage-scoped handles, root-set scene membership, delete-defers-under-pins, cached family flattening with transitive dirty invalidation, cycle refusal (typed error), CoW snapshots under view-protocol rule V5, ratified record/view core (V1-V6) seeding fm-cus. 14 permanent tests: ten scenarios + generational safety + O(touched) snapshot cost + family-cache invalidation + cycle refusal. Miri-clean (all 14 under miri). All gates green; ubs 0 critical.

title: W3: The RecordBuffer — interleaved f32 records, the live-view protocol, and lazy revisioned render mirrors
description:
Implement Marionette's data plane (§8.2, §1.1, D-20). Three layers, one bead, because their contracts interlock:

(1) THE AUTHORITATIVE BUFFER: interleaved f32 records with typed field views, matching the Reference dtypes exactly — [(point f32x3), (rgba f32x4)] for Mobject; the VMobject layout with stroke_rgba/stroke_width/joint_angle/fill_rgba/base_normal/fill_border_width; Surface/DotCloud/Image declaring their own; USER-DECLARED custom dtypes through the schema machinery. This layout is API surface: mobject.data is a zero-copy NumPy structured export (fnp-dtype is a structured-record engine — this is its native representation). Port field-for-field: aligned_data_keys / pointlike_data_keys, data locking as copy-elision, resize-with-interpolation, null-padding family alignment.

(2) THE VIEW PROTOCOL (G0-1-ratified): exported views pin their buffer; resize is copy-on-resize (old views detach with NumPy-natural semantics); mutation through a view marks render state dirty; reallocation under a live view is impossible by construction. While a WRITABLE live view exists, the affected object (or field, where the view is field-scoped) is conservatively invalidated each frame — a live view never silently receives weaker semantics to buy speed. Ranged editing APIs (edit_points(range, ...)-class) exist as the precise-dirty opt-in.

(3) THE RENDER MIRRORS (Rev 4, mandatory): lazy, revisioned struct-of-arrays (or AoSoA sized to the SIMD tier) mirrors of animated fields, materialized at most once per frame — the hot loops (field lerp, transforms, Lumen's IR sync) read mirrors, never the interleaved buffer; synchronization back through the view protocol happens only when a live view or API read demands it.

Acceptance: zero-copy NumPy round-trip tests (via fnp); view-protocol scenario tests (resize under live view, field-scoped dirty, ranged edits); mirror coherence property tests (mirror ≡ buffer at every observation point); mirror laziness tests (untouched fields never materialize); custom-dtype end-to-end test.
close_reason: Data plane landed in fmn-mobject/src/record.rs, three layers: (1) authoritative interleaved f32 buffer with the exact Reference dtypes (Mobject + VMobject field-for-field; byte offsets locked to numpy structured packing by test), custom user dtypes via the schema machinery, aligned/pointlike key sets, lock_data as copy-elision state, resize-with-interpolation ported (incl. single/constant/zero rules), null-padding resize; (2) the ratified view protocol extended with field-scoped views, per-field revisions, conservative invalidation under writable views, and write_range + per-field dirty spans as the precise-dirty opt-in; (3) MirrorSet — lazy revisioned lane-major SoA mirrors with coherence + laziness + conservative-refresh tests. 23 tests green, miri-clean. fnp-backed zero-copy round-trip deferred to fm-n1b (blocked on fm-g2c SUITE.lock).

title: W3: Copy semantics — manim's rules, to the letter
description:
Implement Mobject.copy() to the Reference's exact contract (§8.3) — user code depends on these rules, so they are API semantics, not pixel trivia: recursive copy of submobjects and ndarray attributes; updater CALLABLES kept by reference (not cloned); family-internal attribute references REMAPPED (if self.arrow is also self.submobjects[2], the copy's attribute points at the copy's submobject, not the original's); selected links cleared (parents; scene backrefs) per the Reference's rules.

Also here: the interaction with the arena (copying allocates fresh handles; generation bookkeeping), with the RecordBuffer (deep field copy; live views on the ORIGINAL unaffected; the copy starts with no views), and with target/saved-state machinery (generate_target, save_state / Restore use copies — get the reference topology right or Restore breaks).

The binding tier (fmn-python, W10) layers Python-level copy.copy / copy.deepcopy / pickle onto this engine contract — design the engine-side hooks now (a stable copy-map from old handle → new handle that the Python layer can walk for __dict__ remapping).

Acceptance: fixture tests generated from the Reference's copy behavior on representative object graphs (shared submobjects, attribute aliases, updaters, nested groups); remap-correctness property test (no copy ever references an original's family member); Restore round-trip test; documented rules for which attribute categories copy deep / by-reference / cleared.
close_reason: Copy semantics landed to the letter (§8.3): CopyMap — stable old→new handle map in family (DFS) order from copy_family_mapped, the engine hook fmn-python walks for __dict__ alias remapping (the Reference's family.index rule; order preservation + alias remap verified against a verbatim transcription of mobject.py copy()). Cleared-link rules structural: parents/scene-membership/target/saved_state cleared on every copied member (the stash_mobject_pointers list, exactly). generate_target/save_state/restore_mobject with the Reference's exact cross-link topology (target.saved_state and saved_state.target are SHARED links, order-sensitive — fixtured in both orders). become_mobject (become is a Rust 2024 reserved keyword) over already-aligned families: schema-checked data plane via RecordBuffer::assign_from through the copy-on-resize path (revisions bump past old — bbox signatures/mirrors resync; views detach V6), uniforms+tracker copy, match_updaters root-only; FamilyShapeMismatch names the fm-cye align_family seam. Corpus: gen_copy_fixtures.py transcribes copy()/generate_target/save_state/restore onto mock objects — 21 graph cases, 146 facts, all true; tests/copy_semantics.rs replays them (15 tests) incl. the no-alias-back property over the random corpus, diamond sharing per the ratified G0-1/D-11 family model (Reference would duplicate — documented), live-view isolation, snapshot round-trip of the new links. Accepted ubs findings: test-only panic! guards on malformed fixtures (established idiom, per fm-3xk close). Gates: fmt, check, clippy -D warnings, 364 workspace tests green, crate-DAG OK.

title: W3: Family tree, positional API, bounding boxes, uniforms
description:
Implement the structural/positional surface (§8.4, §1.1): the full positional API — next_to, arrange, arrange_in_grid, to_edge, to_corner, align_to, shift, move_to, center, scale, stretch, set_width/height/depth, match_* — locked by structural fixtures against the Reference (loose f32 tolerances per §16.4, since we compute in f64); bounding boxes with dirty-flag propagation through the family tree (invalidate on point mutation via the RecordBuffer dirty channel, on submobject add/remove, on transform); the cached family flattening with correct invalidation.

Uniforms (§1.1, §8.4): the COMPLETE per-object uniform inventory as typed state — including the four clip-plane slots, stroke_behind, depth-test flag, is_fixed_in_frame (a float mix, per the kept camera model), per-kind AA widths, shading triple (reflectiveness, gloss, shadow) — because scene code reads and writes mobject.uniforms directly (API surface), and Lumen's StyleTable synchronizes from it.

Design care: positional operations are defined against bounding boxes in the Reference; ours must match its box conventions (about_point/about_edge semantics, buff application, stretch vs scale) exactly enough that fixture arrays coincide. Where the Reference's box is subtly wrong (stale caches), ours is correct — any user-visible divergence gets a Behavior Note.

Acceptance: positional fixture corpus green (generated from the pinned Reference across representative shapes/nesting); bbox invalidation property tests (mutation through any channel dirties ancestors exactly once); uniform read/write round-trip via the future Python bridge surface (engine-side test now).
close_reason: The structural/positional surface landed (§8.4, §1.1). Bounding boxes: BoundingBox [min,mid,max] value type with critical-point selection (np.sign semantics — -0.0 selects mid, not min); Stage::get_bounding_box lazily recomputed and keyed by a subtree SIGNATURE (FNV over every family member's point-field revision + identity + len), so a point write (revision bump), a raw RecordBuffer write, a resize, or a structural attach/detach all invalidate automatically through ANY channel with no explicit dirty-flag bookkeeping — the D-20 lazy-revisioned-mirror pattern. materializations counter is the laziness hook. Positional API on Stage (recursing over family exactly like the Reference's Mobject methods over get_family): shift, scale/scale_about, stretch/stretch_about, center, to_edge/to_corner (align_on_border), next_to, move_to, align_to, set_coord/set_x/y/z, rescale_to_fit, set_width/height/depth (+stretch), stretch_to_fit_*, match_width/height/depth/coord/x/y/z, arrange, arrange_in_grid; plus the full critical-point getters (get_center/edge_center/corner/left/right/top/bottom/zenith/nadir, get_coord/x/y/z, length_over_dim/get_width/height/depth, get_start/end). apply_points_function core with about_point/about_edge pivot resolution matching the Reference. Uniforms: complete typed inventory (is_fixed_in_frame as a float MIX not bool, shading triple, four clip-plane slots, anti_alias_width, joint_type enum<->code, flat_stroke, scale_stroke_with_zoom, stroke_behind, depth_test, use_winding_fill) on Entry with read/write accessors + family-recursing setters; carried through copy_family/snapshot/restore. Appendix C rulings: C-2 fixed (get_scale_stroke_with_zoom reads the CORRECT uniform, not flat_stroke) + BN-07 drafted; C-7 (use_winding_fill accepted as an explicit no-op, changes no output bits). Acceptance met: positional fixture corpus green — 26 scenarios generated by scripts/gen_positional_fixtures.py (Reference formulas transcribed from mobject.py into NumPy f64, independent of the Rust engine) rebuilt through the Stage and checked point-for-point + root bbox at loose f32 tol (§16.4); bbox invalidation property tests (lazy: N reads=1 materialization; point-write channel, positional op, leaf-mutation-dirties-ancestor-exactly-once, structural attach/detach); uniform read/write round-trip + recursing-setter family reach; C-2/BN-07 and C-7 tested at both the Uniforms and Stage levels. Gates: fmt --check clean; check --all-targets; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo test green (fmn-mobject: +26-scenario corpus + 8 bbox/uniform property tests + 8 new unit tests; 62 workspace suites, 0 failures); ubs library src panic-free (remaining 3 criticals are test-only panic!/unwrap fixture-integrity guards, same accepted stance as fm-6h6). Points read from the RecordBuffer point field; QuadPath-specific point semantics still land in W7. Follow-ups: fm-ncq (copy semantics to the letter) and fm-yra (updaters/.animate) build on this.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT ADDENDUM — Appendix C rulings owned here: C-2 — the Reference's get_scale_stroke_with_zoom() reads the WRONG uniform (flat_stroke); ours reads the correct scale_stroke_with_zoom uniform (fixed, Behavior-Noted under BN-07). C-7 — use_winding_fill is a documented NO-OP in the Reference (the earclip path is dead in the shipped fill); we ACCEPT the API for compatibility as an explicit no-op (our analytic fill never needed it) — the uniform inventory must include it, accepting writes, affecting nothing, with a doc note. Both get unit tests asserting the ruling (C-2: correct uniform read; C-7: setting it changes no output bits on a fixture scene).

title: W3: The render-order model — z_index, batching partitions, ordering-trace fixtures
description:
Implement the Reference's ACTUAL render-order semantics (§8.5, §1.5): top-level sort by z_index with STABLE scene order for ties; adjacent compatible-item batching; depth-test and fixed-in-frame partitions — kept as semantics, because what draws over what is meaning, not pixels. Lumen consumes this as its draw-order model (§10.1); painter's order for transparent content descends from it (§10.4, §10.8's exact-painter-order command runs).

Deliverable is a specification-with-tests, engine-side: given a scene graph (with shared submobjects, multiple parents, mixed z_index, stroke_behind, depth-tested 3D content, fixed-in-frame overlays), produce the deterministic draw sequence. ORDERING-TRACE FIXTURES pin the tricky cases: a submobject shared by two parents (drawn when? once or twice?), z_index on a child vs parent, stroke_behind interleaving (fill/stroke split ordering), add/remove/bring_to_front/bring_to_back effects on stable order, and the depth-test partition boundary (painter order within non-depth-tested; per-tile depth among depth-tested).

Generate the fixture expectations from the pinned Reference's batching code by INSPECTION (its semantics, not its pixel output), recording each ruling with a source citation — this is the kind of archaeology that must be done once, in one bead, not rediscovered per renderer bug.

Acceptance: the ordering-trace corpus green; property test (stable under no-op mutations; insertion order preserved among equal keys); the written ordering spec committed beside the fixtures (Lumen's W5 beads cite it as normative).

title: W3: Updaters, ValueTrackers, and the .animate builder — corrected where the Reference is buggy
description:
Implement the dynamic-behavior surface (§8.6): insertion-ordered dt and non-dt updaters with suspend/resume; ValueTracker / ExponentialValueTracker / ComplexValueTracker; always_redraw / f_always closure binding into the clock; and the .animate builder implementing the Reference's REAL rules — animation arguments set once per chain; override_animate methods un-chainable; dynamic target lookup at build time.

Two deliberate corrections (D-05; Appendix C rulings — each a Behavior Note): (1) add_updater(call=True) runs the update ONCE (the Reference double-calls — C-5); (2) Group.__add__ returns a NEW group like Mobject.__add__ (the Reference mutates in place on one path — C-6; consistent value semantics).

Semantics that must be exact: updater execution order is insertion order within dt and non-dt groups; the interaction with animation suspend_mobject_updating (an animated mobject's updaters pause unless opted in); updaters observing post-interpolation state at the scene level is CHOREO's frame-order business (§9.3) but the hooks live here; updater add/remove during iteration has a defined outcome (snapshot the list per tick).

Builder mechanics: recording chained calls into a deferred target (this doubles as the Rust fluent API's recording machinery per G0-1 — one implementation, two front doors); prepare_animation accepts Animation | _AnimationBuilder ONLY, rejecting bare methods precisely as the pinned Reference does (§9.1 shares this contract).

Acceptance: ordering tests (mixed dt/non-dt insertion sequences); suspend/resume matrix; the two corrections tested with Behavior Notes drafted; builder fixture corpus (chained-call recording, once-only args, override_animate no-chain, dynamic target).
close_reason: Dynamic-behavior surface landed (§8.6): reworked updater model — one insertion-ordered list mixing typed Dt/NonDt kinds with UpdaterId removal tokens, Reference-exact update order (children first, suspension + no-updaters-in-family pruning), per-tick list snapshot, insert/remove/clear/match, suspend_updating + resume_updating (incl. transitive ancestor clear + call_updater pass); C-5 fixed (call=true runs update(0) exactly once); ValueTracker/ExponentialValueTracker/ComplexValueTracker as typed f64-lane entry state with Reference encodings (ln for exponential; increment = set(get+d) through encoding); always_redraw container-swap binding + f_always; C-6 fixed (group_add always builds a new group); .animate builder with the REAL rules — deferred-command recording (G0-1 shape), args once-per-chain (ArgsAlreadySet), override un-chainable both directions (OverrideNotChainable), build-time target generation + dynamic Mob-target resolution, IntoAnimate as the typed prepare_animation contract (bare methods unrepresentable). BN-07-updater-and-group-fixes.md drafted (C-5, C-6). 18 new tests (dynamics + animate_builder corpora); scenarios updated to the typed updater API. All gates green (297 tests). Note: always_redraw uses container-swap pending fm-ncq's full become; fmn-anim's Animation joins IntoAnimate at fm-67a.

title: W3: Arena snapshots & serialization — SceneState, undo, replay barriers, fixtures
description:
Implement Marionette's persistence layer (§8.7): arena snapshots in the fmn-hash canonical format (§6.7), with per-field .npy export via fnp-io for fixture interchange. Backs four consumers with one mechanism: SceneState (§13.1 — time, play count, top-level mobjects + copies, at the Reference's scope), Studio undo, replay-journal barriers (§13.4 — snapshot at a barrier so replay can restore and continue), and the Gauntlet's geometry-snapshot self-goldens (§16.3 — lifecycle-point snapshots bit-locked per platform).

Scope boundaries stated honestly (§8.7, §13.4): NO claim to serialize Python closures — updater callables snapshot as identity + version hash (the effect model's callback-hash vocabulary); restoring across a code change that alters a callback invalidates the barrier (conservative invalidation is the replay bead's rule; this bead provides the hashes).

Format requirements: versioned schema id per §6.7's policy (additive-minor / breaking-major from day one — snapshots persist in caches and repro bundles); deterministic bytes (canonical field order, NaN/-0 canonicalization at the boundary, no map-order leakage) so that snapshot-hash equality is meaningful for the replay journal and self-goldens; size limits and corruption detection (checksums) on read.

Acceptance: snapshot/restore round-trip across representative scenes (geometry + uniforms + family topology + RNG state via fmn-core); byte-determinism test (same state ⇒ same bytes, twice, and across a re-open); versioning negative test (future-major refused cleanly); .npy field export readable by NumPy in a fixture-generation script.
close_reason: Durable snapshot codec over fmn-hash serial (FMNA/1+2): deterministic bytes, versioned schema, checksum+limits on read, handle re-binding for cross-stage restore, updater identity manifest per the §8.7 honesty clause, SceneState with RNG state words, per-field .npy export. 7 acceptance tests + npy export test; 452 workspace tests green.

title: W4: The Animation contract — constructor surface, lifecycle, lag semantics, prepare_animation
description:
Implement the Animation base contract exactly (§9.1, §1.3): the constructor surface field-for-field — (mobject, run_time=1.0, time_span, lag_ratio, rate_func=smooth, remover, final_alpha_value, suspend_mobject_updating) — the begin → interpolate(alpha) → finish lifecycle, and per-submobject lag via get_sub_alpha (the exact formula: lag distributes alpha across the family by lag_ratio, per the pinned source).

Sharp edges that are contract, not detail: prepare_animation accepts Animation | _AnimationBuilder ONLY and rejects bare methods, precisely as the pinned Reference does (coordinate with W3's builder bead — one recording mechanism serves both front doors); time_span re-windows an animation inside its group interval; final_alpha_value governs the state finish() leaves behind; remover triggers scene removal at finish; suspend_mobject_updating pauses the target's own updaters during the animation unless opted out (interlocks with W3's updater bead and W4's frame-order bead).

Rate-function plumbing: rate_func composes with squish_rate_func and time_span through ONE normalized-alpha pipeline (§9.4) — implement the normalization once, here, so every mechanism family and composition operator shares it; fixture the tricky compositions (squish of squish, time_span inside LaggedStart).

Acceptance: lifecycle-order tests (begin exactly once, interpolate monotone alpha sequence per §9.2's sample points, finish exactly once even under skip); get_sub_alpha fixture corpus generated from the Reference formula across lag_ratio values and family sizes; prepare_animation rejection tests; the normalized-alpha pipeline property tests (composition associativity where defined).
close_reason: Animation contract landed (§9.1): AnimConfig = the §1.3 constructor surface field-for-field (run_time=1.0, time_span, lag_ratio=0, rate_func=smooth, name, remover, final_alpha_value=1.0, suspend_mobject_updating); Animation trait with the Reference lifecycle verbatim — begin (time_span widening -> set_animating_status(true) family+ancestors -> starting copy -> conditional suspend_updating with was-updating memo -> zipped family table (Python-zip truncation) -> interpolate(0)), interpolate -> interpolate_mobject -> per-row interpolate_submobject, finish (final_alpha_value -> status clear -> conditional resume with C-5 one-call pass); subclass extension via setup() hook so the canonical order is written once. The ONE normalized-alpha pipeline (§9.4): time_spanned_alpha and sub_alpha as free fns, formula-for-formula, with RateFunc as composable data (Base/Squish/Scaled) so squish-of-squish is representable+journalable; fixture corpus (5292 sub_alpha + 420 time_spanned cases) generated from the pinned formulas. prepare_animation typed: IntoAnimation for Animation types + AnimBuilder + BuiltAnimate only (bare method = compile error), MethodAnimation as the .animate carrier (source->target f64 lerp; PathArcUnsupported + UnalignedFamilies named errors as fm-cye boundaries). Stage grew set_animating_status/is_animating/is_changing/update_mobject (committed by the concurrent agent as d3c6135). BN-08 documents the three deliberate divergences (update_rate_info explicit zeros, InvalidTimeSpan named error, typed rejection). 24 new contract tests + 2 stage tests; all gates green (fmt, check, clippy -D, tests, DAG, ubs — 3 accepted test-only panics on malformed-fixture guards).

title: W4: The RationalFrameClock — manim's exact sample points, drift-free
description:
Implement the clock (§9.2, D-07, BN-02): an i64 frame index over fps, computing time as exact rationals — emitting the SAME nominal sample sequence as the Reference's arange(0, run_time, 1/fps) + 1/fps semantics: no emitted alpha-zero progression frame (begin() interpolates at zero separately, outside the emission sequence); upward duration rounding (a run_time that is not a whole number of frames still covers its end); the final sample may exceed run_time (alpha clamps per the Reference's rule); finish() applies final_alpha_value. Scene code experiences manim's timing model without manim's float drift — hour-long renders accumulate zero error (Behavior Note BN-02).

Why rational: the Reference advances a float accumulator; at 30fps an hour of frames drifts visibly, and two runs disagree with themselves under different chunkings. Our clock is (frame_index, fps) — time derives, never accumulates. Every subsystem that needs "now" (updaters' dt, wait_until predicates, presenter holds, A/V sync in Reel) reads this clock; dt is exactly 1/fps as a rational, converted to f64 at the consumer boundary.

PERMANENT REFUSAL (D-18, §10.5): adaptive or variable frame sampling is refused forever — the sample points are semantics, and every scheduler freedom in §17 is defined relative to them. Encode this in the type: there is no API to emit off-grid samples.

Acceptance: sample-sequence fixtures vs the Reference formula across awkward run_times (0.1, 1/3, 2.9999, exact multiples); drift test (10^6 frames: exact equality with closed-form time); wait/wait_until/skip interaction tests; the no-off-grid-API property documented and asserted.
close_reason: RationalFrameClock landed in fmn-anim: RationalTime (frames/fps — off-grid time unrepresentable by construction, D-18 refusal encoded in the type), FrameSegment sampler with the Reference's exact emission semantics (no alpha-zero frame, upward duration rounding via exact-rational ceil on the received f64, alpha clamp, skip = whole segment), overflow-free exact comparisons vs f64 seconds. 9 acceptance tests: count fixtures cross-checked vs Python Fraction AND numpy arange (BN-02 divergence set: 0.1@30fps = 4 vs Python's 3, documented), coverage property over 20k durations ((n-1)/fps < rt <= n/fps exactly), million-frame drift test (bit-equal to closed form; float accumulator provably drifts), wait/wait_until/skip interaction, refused inputs (0 fps, NaN/inf, overlong). BN-02 drafted at docs/behavior_notes/BN-02-rational-clock.md.

title: W4: The six-step frame order + the FramePacket freeze
description:
Implement the frame loop's load-bearing order EXACTLY (§9.3, §1.4, D-19): (1) animation update_mobjects(dt) → (2) animation interpolate(alpha) → (3) time advances → (4) SCENE updaters run, observing post-interpolation state → (5) capture → (6) emit. always_redraw-style scenes semantically depend on step 4 seeing step 2's output; this order is a contract, not an implementation choice.

The UPDATE-ORDER CORPUS locks it: dt-updaters on animated mobjects; target-with-updater; suspend both ways (animation suspending target updaters; explicit suspend_updating); wait and wait_until; skip mode; final samples; unequal run_times in one play(); time_span re-windowing. Each corpus scene asserts observable state at specific frames — these are semantic regression tests, not pixel tests.

THE FRAMEPACKET (Rev 4's boundary at step 5, reordering nothing): capture freezes an immutable FramePacket — frame index and alpha, camera state, RNG substream states (via §6.5's keyed forks), and revisioned references into Lumen's compiled render state — after which the frame no longer depends on mutable scene state. Everything before the freeze is the serial front-end; everything after (IR sync, binning, raster, color conversion, emission) consumes ONLY the packet. This is what makes the §17.4 pipeline and §9.5 frame-parallelism legal. Design the packet as genuinely immutable (no interior mutability, revision handles not live pointers) and cheap (CoW snapshot references, not deep copies).

Acceptance: the update-order corpus green; FramePacket immutability enforced by construction (API review + a test that mutating scene state after capture cannot affect a held packet); packet-cost measurement (O(touched-state), suitable for 3-6 in flight per §17.4).
close_reason: Six-step frame order + FramePacket freeze landed (§9.3, D-19): play_segment/wait_segment in fmn-anim drive the load-bearing order exactly — per-animation update_mobjects(dt) interleaved with interpolate(t/run_time, raw-unclamped so the §9.4 pipeline clips unequal run_times), time advance (clock + Stage::update fixed to advance its float mirror BEFORE updaters, matching increment_time-then-update_mobjects), scene updaters over post-interpolation state, capture, emit. Skip = one big-dt step, no capture/emit; BN-10 corrects the Reference's skip-mode double-dt (finish pass is dt=0 in both modes, so skipped and played segments end in identical updater state — corpus-locked). Reference begin_animations (roots unrooted animated mobjects) and finish_animations (remover cleanup + dt=0 pass) kept. FramePacket: immutable by construction — frame index, segment frame, clamped alpha, exact RationalTime, Rc'd CoW snapshot (O(touched) verified via storage_id sharing), and DERIVED rng: keyed per-frame forks are pure fns of (seed, substream, frame_index) so the packet carries two words and rng_fork() reproduces any substream exactly (D-18 property). Camera state + IR revisions join at fm-gw7/fm-5xm (documented subset). 16-scene update-order corpus green: post-interpolation observation (always_redraw property), time-before-updaters, suspend matrix, copy ticking, skip-parity, unequal run_times, final-sample clamp, time_span re-window, wait/wait_until/skipped-wait, membership edges, packet isolation/cost/rng. 339 workspace tests; all gates green (ubs: 0 critical).

title: W4: Five mechanism families → the 80 animation classes + the align_data fixture corpus
description:
Implement the animation catalog the way the plan factors it (§9.4, §1.3): FIVE mechanism families implemented once, well — (1) Transform-family: family alignment + field lerp through path_func (straight, arc, clockwise/counterclockwise variants); (2) partial-reveal (ShowCreation/Uncreate/Write/DrawBorderThenFill, driven by true proportion via W2's arclength layer); (3) fade/grow (with shift/scale composition, FadeTransform's cross-fade mechanics); (4) indication composites (Indicate, Flash, FocusOn, ApplyWave, WiggleOutThenIn, passing-flash family); (5) functional maps (Homotopy, ComplexHomotopy, PhaseFlow, MoveAlongPath — the latter constant-speed by W2ARC, BN-03). Then ALL 80 classes from Appendix A's animation modules as parameterizations of those mechanisms, each mapped in the Parity Ledger — including the transform zoo (ApplyMethod/ApplyMatrix/ApplyComplexFunction/CyclicReplace/Swap/Restore...), creation/fading/growing/indication/movement/numbers/rotation/specialized/update modules, and TurnInsideOut with C-1's ruling (the Reference calls a nonexistent method; we implement the evident intent — Behavior Note).

THE ALIGN CORPUS: the align_data family-alignment path (null-padding, point resampling, family-shape reconciliation between source and target) under a 1000-pair fixture corpus — STRUCTURAL assertions: point counts, family shapes, interpolation endpoints — generated across the mobject-type matrix (VMobject×VMobject with differing subpath counts, group×single, text×shape...). Alignment bugs are the classic manim wart; the corpus is the wall against regressions.

Acceptance: mechanism unit tests per family; all 80 classes constructible with Reference-default parameters and Ledger-mapped; the align corpus green; TransformMatchingParts/Shapes present (TransformMatchingTex/Strings land with W6's span maps — leave the seam explicit).
