The two-stage audio mux's stage 2 (-c:v copy -c:a aac -map ...) must not re-encode video (copy verified in the fake-ffmpeg contract). --prerun frame counting and insert-file semantics get their own fake-ffmpeg scenarios.

title: W8: The ordered asynchronous emitter — sequence-numbered frames, preallocated rings, never a stall
description:
Implement Reel's orchestration core (§14.1, D-19): sequence-numbered frames from the pipeline (§17.4) and from frame-parallel pure segments (§9.5) publish IN FRAME-INDEX ORDER through a preallocated ring of frame slots — Lumen never stalls on a sink, and no sink ever observes an out-of-order or torn frame.

Mechanics: a bounded ring sized by the RAM-derived, config-visible in-flight budget (PG-6: each in-flight 4K RGBA frame ≈ 33MB — the budget is real memory); workers deposit completed frames by index; the emitter drains the contiguous prefix to sinks (file writers, the ffmpeg pipe, the Studio stream, the y4m/PNG-sequence writers); backpressure propagates to the pipeline when the ring fills (bounded, deadlock-free: the ring never blocks a frame that the drain is waiting on — slot reservation happens at frame dispatch); the hot path never allocates, resizes, or synchronously flushes a frame-sized buffer (§14.3's rule).

Ordering is a determinism obligation, not a UX nicety (§10.5 e): frame-index-ordered emission is one of the parallelism contract's six clauses — the frame-parallel equivalence tests (W4PURITY, PG-5) depend on this component being airtight. Multi-sink: a render may feed video + PNG sequence + preview simultaneously; each sink observes the same ordered stream; a slow sink applies backpressure rather than dropping (drops are an explicit per-sink policy, only for preview-class sinks).

Acceptance: out-of-order deposit tests (adversarial completion orders ⇒ in-order emission, always); backpressure test (slow sink throttles pipeline without deadlock); torn-frame impossibility test (a slot is never visible to sinks mid-write — API review + concurrency test under the thread sweep); multi-sink consistency test; ring-budget accounting test vs the configured in-flight limit.

title: W8: fmn-cache — the content-addressed store
description:
Implement the persistent cache (§14.4): content-addressed on fmn-hash — atomic writes (write-temp + rename, never partial visibility), cross-process locks (two fmn invocations share a cache safely), per-entry checksums verified on read, versioned namespaces (a schema/version bump invalidates cleanly without clearing unrelated namespaces), corruption recovery (a bad entry is evicted and recomputed, never trusted, never fatal), traversal protection (keys never map to paths outside the cache root), defined eviction (LRU-class with pinning for in-use entries), size ceilings (config-visible).

Serves three consumers with one discipline: TYPESET RESULTS (fmd-math + text layout, keyed on string + preamble-pack hash + font hashes + engine version — W6TEX's contract; the dominant win, PG-7's <100µs cached path); FETCHED ASSETS (through the AssetFetcher capability — cached by content hash with the fetch policy recorded); THE REPLAY JOURNAL's storage (§13.4 — journal segments and checkpoint snapshots live under a cache namespace with pinning while a session is live).

Determinism interaction: a cache is an OPTIMIZATION, never an oracle — certified renders must produce identical output with a cold or warm cache (the key includes everything semantic, so a hit is definitionally equivalent; test it), and --clear-cache (the Reference flag, kept) must be safe at any moment.

Acceptance: atomicity tests (kill -9 mid-write leaves a consistent store); cross-process lock test (two writers, no corruption); corruption-injection tests (flipped bytes detected, evicted, recomputed); eviction/ceiling tests with pinning; cold-vs-warm equivalence test on a typeset-heavy scene; traversal fuzz on keys.

title: W8: The sound mixer — WAV/PCM with a fully specified matrix
description:
Implement audio (§14.5; displaces pydub): the WAV/PCM mixer with EVERY behavior specified — sample formats (i16/i24/f32) and rates with a NAMED resampler (documented algorithm and quality), channel layouts (mono/stereo mapping rules), gain semantics (gain, gain_to_background — the Reference's ducking parameter), negative time offsets (sound starting before its add point clips correctly), overlay-past-end (the mix extends), clipping policy (defined: clamp with a warning counter, not wraparound), dithering policy on bit-depth reduction, DETERMINISTIC MIX ORDER (sounds mix in a defined order so float summation is reproducible — certified WAV output is bit-locked), and A/V sync on the rational clock (sample-accurate placement from frame times — no float drift between audio and video, BN-02's audio corollary).

Scope boundaries: WAV in/out is native (W8CODEC2); NON-WAV inputs (mp3, etc.) decode through the ffmpeg capability boundary with named capability errors when absent (D2); certified output is WAV (ffmpeg's encoded audio is uncertified like all its products).

Scene surface: Scene.add_sound with the Reference's signature and semantics; the two-stage mux (W8FFMPEG) consumes the mixed WAV.

Acceptance: mix golden vectors (offsets, overlaps, gains, negative offsets, past-end) bit-locked; resampler quality tests (documented passband/stopband vs spec); clipping/dither policy tests; determinism test (same inputs ⇒ same WAV bytes, any thread count); A/V sync test (a click on frame N lands at the sample-exact time); ffmpeg-decode capability path test with the fake-ffmpeg.

title: W9: The Scene runtime — the state machine on the rational clock
description:
Implement Scene (§13.1, §1.4): the full runtime surface — construct; add/remove/bring_to_front/bring_to_back/clear with W3ORDER's stable-order semantics; play/wait/wait_until on the rational clock through W4's engine (the six-step order and FramePacket are Choreo's; the orchestration — begin/progress/finish per play, pre_play/post_play hooks — is here); SceneState snapshot/restore at the Reference's scope (time, play count, top-level mobjects + copies — via W3SNAP); EndScene; ThreeDScene defaults (camera orientation, ambient rotation hooks); presenter mode / hold loops; show(); BlankScene; the windowed fps=30 override (§1.4's quirk, kept as config semantics).

Also here: the typesetting PREFLIGHT hook (W6TEX's pre-play walk — the scene runtime exposes the constructed-mobject walk before first play); skip-mode (-s) and range flags (-n) semantics — skip renders nothing but advances state EXACTLY (updaters and RNG advance identically so post-skip state matches played state — a determinism property worth its own tests); frame-capture wiring to Reel's emitter.

The runtime is the integration point where Choreo/Marionette/Lumen/Reel meet — most of this bead's value is the CORPUS: end-to-end scene tests (our own primitive corpus, ~25 public scenes per G1) exercising the full loop with self-golden frame hashes.

Acceptance: scene lifecycle tests (construct/play/wait/state round-trips); skip-equivalence test (skip-to-end state ≡ play-to-end state, bit-for-bit on the arena snapshot); SceneState restore tests; ThreeDScene default fixtures; the primitive corpus running end-to-end with self-goldens locked.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT ADDENDUM — Reference runtime classes this bead displaces or owns (census rows): SceneFileWriter — its user-facing semantics (output file naming, --file_name / --video_dir interaction, partial-movie/subdivide file conventions, the open-file-on-finish behavior behind -o) are kept as CLI-observable behavior; the implementation is Reel's (W8EMIT/W8FFMPEG) — this bead owns the naming/paths contract + fixtures. ModuleLoader + extract_scene (BlankScene included) — scene discovery and selection semantics (how a file's Scene subclasses are enumerated, -n selection, --write_all ordering) reproduced for the Python front door via fmn-python and mirrored for Rust scene registration. The windowed fps=30 override (§1.4) is config semantics owned here. Acceptance additions: output-naming fixture matrix (scene name x flags x formats); scene-enumeration/selection fixtures matching Reference behavior; BlankScene smoke test.

title: W9: Events + InteractiveScene — the dispatcher and the interaction surface
description:
Implement interactivity (§13.2, §1.4): the event dispatcher (EventDispatcher/EventListener/EventType — registration, propagation, listener ordering per the Reference) and InteractiveScene's behaviors: selection (click/drag-rect selection of mobjects, selection highlight rectangles), grab/move, resize, color-picking, clipboard copy/paste of mobjects, keyboard-map COMPATIBLE with the Reference's bindings (the muscle-memory surface: users' hands know these keys).

Consumers: the Studio (W9STUDIO) forwards browser/TUI input events into this dispatcher; the interactive control mobjects (W7DETEX's Button/Checkbox/sliders/ControlPanel) register listeners here; MotionMobject/Textbox behaviors.

Design care: events interleave with the frame loop at a DEFINED point (they run in the serial front-end, before capture — they are scene-state mutations and pipeline barriers when they must observe pixels per §13.4); event handling in a paused/presenter-hold state has defined semantics; the dispatcher is deterministic under replay (the journal records event streams with timestamps on the rational clock — replaying a session re-fires events identically, §13.4's contract).

Acceptance: dispatcher unit tests (registration/removal/ordering/propagation); InteractiveScene behavior tests against scripted event streams (select → drag → resize → color → paste, asserting scene-state outcomes); keyboard-map fixture vs the Reference's bindings; replay determinism test (recorded event stream re-applied ⇒ identical final state); frame-loop interleaving test (events never mutate mid-capture).

title: W9: Supervisor + scene-worker iteration — rebuild, restart, restore, replay (no dlopen)
description:
Implement live iteration (§13.3, D-14): the supervisor + scene-worker-subprocess architecture — code change detected → incremental rebuild → worker restart → restore nearest serialized checkpoint → replay journal from the last valid barrier. Crashes isolate to the worker (a panic in scene code never takes down the Studio or supervisor); the supervisor owns the UI connection, the build pipeline, and checkpoint storage; the worker owns scene execution and rendering. No dlopen, no hot-patching — process isolation is the reliability story AND the security story (untrusted content renders only in the isolated worker, §13.5).

Protocol: a versioned supervisor↔worker IPC (fmn-hash canonical serialization) carrying: scene enumeration, play/seek/scrub commands, frame streams (shared-memory or pipe per platform via fmn-platform), checkpoint push/restore, journal segments, crash reports (a worker crash yields a structured report + the journal tail — every crash is a replayable bug per §18's repro-bundle story).

Edit-to-frame is PG-4's second budget (<1s on a trailing edit of a 30s pure scene): the mechanism is checkpoint restore + journal replay (only the edited tail re-executes) + warm caches (typeset/compiled paths survive restart via fmn-cache). fmn-python preserves the literal IPython embed()/checkpoint_paste() workflow (W10's bridge exposes the hooks; the checkpoint machinery is here).

Acceptance: crash-isolation tests (worker panic → supervisor alive → auto-restart → checkpoint restore); rebuild-restart-restore cycle test with a scripted edit (measured against the PG-4 budget on the pinned profile); IPC protocol golden tests + version-skew rejection; checkpoint round-trip fidelity (restored state bit-equals pre-restart snapshot); an end-to-end autoreload session test.

title: W9: The replay journal + effect model — record everything, replay cheaply, invalidate conservatively
description:
Implement replay (§13.4, R16): the journal records COMMANDS AND EFFECTS — play/wait/add/remove operations, RNG substream states at barriers, camera/audio state, content hashes of every file/font/asset read, callback version hashes, ffmpeg invocations — with OPAQUE BARRIERS for non-replayable operations and CONSERVATIVE invalidation (when in doubt, a barrier).

Three consumers, one record: (1) the supervisor's edit-replay (W9SUPER: restore checkpoint, replay journal from the last valid barrier — entries whose recorded hashes still match replay from cache; a changed callback hash invalidates from that point); (2) the PURITY CLASSIFIER's evidence (§9.5: the effect model IS the classifier's vocabulary — segments whose recorded effects are pure are frame-parallel eligible; classifications are journaled per segment, R20); (3) the PIPELINE's synchronization vocabulary (§17.4: any operation that must observe rendered pixels is a pipeline barrier that drains in-flight frames; ordinary manim scene code never hits one — assert this on the corpus).

Soundness doctrine (R16): replay-cache unsoundness is the failure mode — the design answer is conservative invalidation everywhere: unrecognized effects are opaque; divergent operation classes become barriers BY DEFAULT; a replay that would diverge (hash mismatch mid-replay) falls back to full re-execution, correctly, silently, and records why.

Also here: the one-command REPRO BUNDLE (§18) — scene + input closure packaged so every bug report is a deterministic replay — and the journal's appearance in provenance sidecars.

Acceptance: record/replay equivalence tests (replayed session ⇒ bit-identical final state + frames on pure content); invalidation tests (edited callback → replay stops at the right barrier); opaque-barrier tests (an unrecognized effect forces re-execution, never wrong reuse); purity-evidence round-trip with W4PURITY; pipeline-barrier drain test; a repro-bundle end-to-end test (bundle → fresh machine sim → identical render).
close_reason: Replay journal + effect model + repro bundle landed in fmn-scene (FMNA/3+4). Command/effect/asset/subprocess/checkpoint records; plan_replay with conservative invalidation naming its reason; ReplayAudit divergence fallback; EffectClass embedding W4 purity evidence (R20); PixelObserving pipeline-barrier vocabulary with corpus assertion; Custom→Opaque coercion; §18 repro bundle with closure verification. 10 acceptance tests incl. bit-identical fresh-stage replay and bundle end-to-end. 538 workspace tests green.

title: W9: The Studio — preview server, inspector, scrubbing, TUI, security model
description:
Implement the Studio (§13.5, D-14, leapfrog #6): localhost with an EXPLICIT security model — loopback-only binding, per-request capability token, Host/Origin validation, size limits, no filesystem serving beyond embedded UI assets, event-rate limits, session expiry. Untrusted content renders only in the isolated worker (W9SUPER).

Streams: baseline is MULTIPART PNG (we own PNG — no dependency, works everywhere); MJPEG as the ffmpeg-accelerated option; browser-local WASM rendering is the endgame (G5). On supported Apple silicon the preview renders through the METAL ANNEX by default (W5METAL — speed welcome, bit promises irrelevant for preview) with the CPU fallback declared (OQ-12/G3's rule).

Surfaces: TIMELINE SCRUBBING over W4TIMELINE + checkpoints (seek anywhere; pure segments reconstruct instantly, stateful segments snap to checkpoints); THE INSPECTOR — family tree, live record fields (the RecordBuffer's typed views), uniforms, TYPESET SPAN MAPS (W6SPAN's in-Studio payoff: hover a glyph, see its source span); DEBUG OVERLAYS — tiles, control cages, bounds, winding, depth (§18); a kitty/sixel TUI in the fp-frankentui lineage for SSH sessions (leapfrog #5's terminal leg).

Embedded UI: assets compiled into the binary (no runtime file serving, no CDN — the one-binary story extends to the Studio).

Acceptance: security tests (non-loopback refused; missing/stale token refused; oversized/flooding events limited; no path escapes); stream conformance (multipart PNG consumable by stock browsers); scrub correctness tests (seek(t) state matches play-through, via W4TIMELINE's machinery); inspector data-accuracy tests against engine state; TUI smoke test over a pty; annex-preview fallback test.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT ADDENDUM — the browser-local WASM endgame (§13.5) is now explicitly staged: the serialized-timeline PLAYER is fm-oee (W5 WASM tier 2 — Studio gains 'export interactive player bundle'); browser-local LIVE rendering (the Studio UI rendering frames client-side via the tier-1 renderer instead of streaming PNG) is the G5-era follow-on layered on fm-oee + fm-l97 — file its bead when scheduled. The multipart-PNG baseline remains the compatibility floor forever (works with zero client capabilities).

title: W9: The fmn CLI + batch + doctor — the Reference's flags where they still mean something
description:
Implement the CLI (§13.6): the Reference's flag surface kept where it still means something — write/skip/quality/resolution (the resolution table + --uhd etc.), open (-o), fullscreen, presenter, GIF, transparent, --vcodec/--pix_fmt (feeding W8FFMPEG's negotiation), quiet, write-all, scene/range selection (-n), embed-line (-e), --subdivide, --file_name, --prerun, --video_dir, --config_file, --clear-cache, --autoreload, background color — with EXIT CODES and FLAG INTERACTIONS specified in the API schema (W10SCHEMA generates the CLI table; drift is a build error). TeX-toolchain configuration is replaced by fmd-math PACK selection; --reproducible selects certified determinism (and implies the certified engine + canonical outputs + sidecar manifest).

FMN BATCH: multi-scene farms under asupersync's batch feature (§2.7 — the ONLY place asupersync appears; never in the frame loop) with structured cancellation, budgets, per-scene manifests, and a machine-readable batch report.

FMN DOCTOR: capabilities report — ffmpeg fingerprint (path + content hash + version) and its hardware-encoder inventory (§14.3), bundled + user fonts, cache state, the derived ExecutionPlan (§17.4/§18: topology, teams, tiers), determinism mode support per platform. Robot-mode NDJSON per AGENTS.md's agent-ergonomics rules (stable versioned schema, explicit exit codes, no human decoration in machine output).

Acceptance: flag-parity fixtures vs the schema (every kept flag: parse, interact, exit codes); an end-to-end render invocation test matrix (formats x quality x reproducible) against fake-ffmpeg + native sinks; batch cancellation/budget tests under the asupersync lab; doctor output golden tests (robot mode schema-validated); --clear-cache safety test.

title: W10: The one API schema — classes, methods, defaults, flags, config keys; codegen for both front doors
description:
Implement the single source of API truth (§16.2, D-05 adjacency): ONE machine-readable schema describing classes, methods, parameters, defaults, CLI flags, and config keys — from which are GENERATED: Rust config structs + builders (the kwargs displacement: Default structs + builder methods, §15.1), fmn-python signatures (argument names/defaults/keyword-ness matching the pinned manimlib surface), Parity Ledger rows (W10LEDGER consumes), CLI flag tables (W9CLI consumes), and docs. DRIFT BETWEEN FRONT DOORS IS A BUILD ERROR — the schema check runs in CI; a Rust builder or Python signature that disagrees with the schema fails the build.

Bootstrapping: extract the initial schema from the pinned Reference (signatures, defaults, keyword arguments) via the W1GAUNT extraction tooling; then the schema is AUTHORED (our canonical names per C-9's typo rulings — tickness_multiplier et al. get canonical names in the schema with exact-name aliases in fmn-python), reviewed, and versioned. Include the wildcard-export inventory (§1.6: from manimlib import * has no authoritative __all__ — the Ledger enumerates SYMBOLS, and the schema is where that enumeration lives).

Also here: fmn-config's struct migration to codegen (W1CONF hand-wrote; this bead swaps in generated types), and the CLI exit-code/flag-interaction table (W9CLI's normative source).

Acceptance: schema file + generators in CI; drift negative tests (mutate a generated signature → build fails); import-surface conformance harness comparing the schema's export list against the actual pinned wildcard namespace; C-9 alias tests; docs generation smoke test.

title: W10: fmn-python — the manimlib bridge with real Python object semantics
description:
Implement the Python front door (§15.2, D-03's sibling promise; ratified by G0-5): a PyO3 module presenting the pinned manimlib module surface with NORMAL Python object semantics — subclassing every exported base with real MRO/override dispatch (a subclass overriding init_points/interpolate is CALLED BACK by the engine at the right lifecycle points); writable __dict__ and arbitrary attributes participating in copy remapping (W3COPY's copy-map hooks); custom data_dtypes through the RecordBuffer schema machinery; properties/descriptors; live data/uniforms/submobjects mutation under §8.2's view protocol (zero-copy NumPy structured views against the engine's buffers); copy.copy/deepcopy/pickle; weakrefs/identity/hashing across engine collection; exception mapping both directions; GIL and reentrancy rules documented and enforced; cross-thread restrictions explicit.

This is the ONE crate allowed project-authored unsafe (PyO3 expansion — D3); keep the surface thin and mechanical, with engine logic staying in the safe crates.

The promise, stated honestly (§15.2): source compatibility and correct, beautiful output — EXPLICITLY not frame reproduction; Python scenes are Python programs (may import real NumPy, do I/O); certification covers them only as far as the input closure captures (§15.5).

The IPython workflow: embed() and checkpoint_paste() preserved literally (W9SUPER's checkpoint machinery + the bridge's REPL hooks); autoreload semantics via the supervisor.

Acceptance: G0-5's spike suite promoted to the permanent bridge test suite and extended per the schema (every exported base subclassable; every lifecycle callback fires); view-protocol conformance from Python (resize/detach semantics observed NumPy-side); copy/deepcopy/pickle round-trips; import-surface conformance green (W10SCHEMA's harness); wheel-buildable (W11 packages; this bead makes it importable).

title: W10: The binding-tax program — batched crossings, GIL discipline, the opt-in acceleration ladder
description:
Implement §15.2's Rev-4 performance program for the Python boundary — arbitrary Python callbacks are the ONE serial, GIL-sensitive component no scheduler can dissolve, so the tax is engineered down and made visible:

MECHANISMS: method-resolution and callback handles CACHED (resolve once per (type, method), invalidate on class mutation); native→Python crossings BATCHED wherever semantics allow (updater groups crossing once per frame, not once per field); the GIL RELEASED across all native compilation, rasterization, conversion, and output waits (the interpreter and rasterizer overlap instead of alternating — §17.4's pipeline overlaps rendering with subsequent Python frame construction); dirty propagation batched once per callback group rather than per field write.

THE ACCELERATION LADDER (opt-in, never silent): above the always-correct Python updater sit explicit substitutions with identical semantics for their declared class — batched Python updater (fewer crossings) → array updater (one vectorized RecordBuffer operation) → native updater (Rust/engine-executable). Each is an EXPLICIT opt-in with documented semantics; silent substitution is prohibited (D-05's spirit: labeled, never silent).

VISIBILITY: callback-heavy scenes are DETECTED and reported with their phase breakdown (§17.1's Python-callback phase) so the tax is visible before anyone optimizes around it; PG-8's class table — native built-ins ≤1.10x Rust; per-frame-callback, point-transform-callback, and dynamic-subclass classes each with a PUBLISHED budget — is measured by W10PERF's rig against G0-5's baseline numbers.

Acceptance: crossing-count instrumentation + reduction tests (batching measurably reduces crossings on the callback corpus); GIL-release verification (a Python thread makes progress during a long native render); ladder correctness tests (each rung bit-equals the Python rung on its declared class); the detection report golden test; PG-8 baseline numbers recorded.

title: W10: VIDEO_CORPUS.lock — the pinned scene gallery, shims, and the adapter shortlist
description:
Implement the corpus gate's machinery (§15.3-15.4, re-scoped by Rev 3): VIDEO_CORPUS.lock pins an EXACT scene allowlist from the 3b1b videos tree — scene names, helper-module commits, asset manifest — under the CC BY-NC-SA fixture policy (private gallery fixtures, public permissive primitive corpus, per-scene attribution).

THE G4a CRITERION (this bead builds the harness; the gate bead asserts it): the enumerated scenes run with SOURCE UNEDITED under the documented shims — import virtualization (manimlib import surface via fmn-python), asset-path virtualization (corpus assets resolve through the AssetFetcher/cache), fonts (bundled faces substituting for the era's system fonts, documented) — and pass STRUCTURAL assertions (object counts, timings, bounding envelopes) plus Look-Gallery review (human-judged visual quality). NOT pixel diffs — that apparatus is deleted by design (§4). Scenes whose TeX outruns the current fmd-math tier are marked PENDING-WITH-NAMED-CONSTRUCTS, feeding the W6RATCHET (the corpus is the ratchet's real-world half).

CUSTOM GLSL, scoped honestly (§15.4, D-13): set_color_by_code and custom shader folders are EXCLUDED from the compatibility claim (Strategy A wording in the Ledger); a SHORT list of known-corpus native adapters (Strategy B: the fractal-uniform protocols — Mandelbrot/Newton scenes) maintained only as far as the gallery allowlist needs them (OQ-9 resolves here: keep adapters or ship exclusions, per what the allowlist actually contains); the restricted-GLSL interpreter stays banked (OQ-8).

Era handling (R13): out-of-era scenes excluded per-scene with recorded reasons; era shims documented per scene.

Acceptance: the lock file + fetch/verify tooling; the shim layer with per-shim docs; the structural-assertion harness running against at least a seed subset; the pending-constructs report wired to the ratchet; the adapter decision recorded (OQ-9).

title: W10: The Parity Ledger + Behavior Notes — symbol-granular, semantics-tiered, user-facing
description:
Implement the two user-facing truth registers:

THE PARITY LEDGER (§16.1): rows are SYMBOLS AND BEHAVIORS — module · symbol · kind (class/method/property/function/constant/CLI flag/config key) · wildcard-exported? · signature/defaults · semantic status: SAME | IMPROVED (Behavior-Note link) | TIERED (out-of-tier ledger link, §16.6) | EXCLUDED (e.g. custom GLSL) · tests · notes. Covers the utility-function surface (the wildcard-import namespace with no authoritative __all__ — §1.6), the CLI, and config keys. Generated FROM the W10SCHEMA plus per-symbol status annotations; regenerates the coverage badge; CI FAILS ON REGRESSION (a symbol dropping status is a build break with a required written amendment).

THE BEHAVIOR NOTES (§16.8, D-05): the user-facing register of deliberate differences from classic manim — one evidence-backed entry each, written as MIGRATION GUIDANCE, not apologia. Seed set: BN-01 single RNG; BN-02 rational clock; BN-03 true arc length; BN-04 color (linear-light + familiar gradients); BN-05 native typesetting metrics; BN-06 renderer (analytic coverage, round caps, arclength width); BN-07 Reference bugs fixed (Appendix C rulings C-1..C-9); BN-08 de-TeX'd classes; BN-09 one arc-density rule. Individual notes are drafted in their owning beads; THIS bead owns the register's format, review bar (each note: what changed, why, what you'll observe, how to adapt), publication (docs artifact), and completeness check (every IMPROVED Ledger row links a note; CI-verified).

THE OUT-OF-TIER LEDGER (§16.6): the honest fringe — complex-script shaping, CFF, variable fonts, exotic mesh/media formats, arbitrary GLSL — each with rationale and a REVISIT TRIGGER.

Acceptance: Ledger generation + badge in CI with regression blocking; the notes register published with the seed set complete and cross-linked; out-of-tier ledger with triggers; a completeness CI check (no IMPROVED without a note; no TIERED without an out-of-tier row).

title: W10: The full Gauntlet corpora — correctness oracles, Look Gallery, engine-equivalence, fuzzing campaign
description:
Scale W1GAUNT's rig into the full verification machine (§16.3-16.5):

CORRECTNESS ORACLES: analytic ground truths — arc length vs closed forms; boolean identities; winding invariants; TeX layout vs the published Appendix-G parameters; color-model round-trips — plus property/metamorphic tests RESTRICTED TO VALID LAWS (integer-pixel translation equivariance; stateless resampling; subdivision invariance) and the structural-fixture corpus against the Reference where formulas intentionally coincide (loose f32, §16.4).

SELF-GOLDENS at scale: geometry snapshots at lifecycle points + frame hashes for the primitive (~25 scenes, public) and feature corpora — bit-locked per platform, cross-platform under certified; THE merge blocker (D-16).

THE LOOK GALLERY: side-by-side FrankenManim vs captured Reference imagery, human-reviewed, with perceptual metrics (SSIM, edge-distance, local-error percentiles) as SMOKE ALARMS, never hard gates; verdict vocabulary at-least-as-good / different-but-fine (Behavior-Noted) / regression (fix); review workflow tooling (render pairs, record verdicts, track regressions to their introducing change).

THE ENGINE-EQUIVALENCE SUITE (Rev 4, §16.3): fast-CPU and annex engines vs certified-engine reference frames under the VERSIONED visual-equivalence budget (perceptual + max-error bounds) — BLOCKING for engine changes, informational elsewhere.

THE FUZZING CAMPAIGN (§16.5): SVG, TTF, YAML-subset, TeX strings, PNG/JPEG, the canonical deserializer — with RESOURCE-BUDGET assertions (error within budget; never hang; never overallocate); fmd-math fuzzing (arbitrary token streams error precisely, never garble); CI-scheduled continuous fuzz with corpus persistence.

Acceptance: all four planes running in CI with their blocking semantics wired correctly (goldens block always; equivalence blocks engine paths; Gallery informs; fuzz runs scheduled); the oracle corpus documented per §16.3's taxonomy; a demonstrated regression drill for each plane (inject → detect → block/report).
notes: fmn-hash exposes fmn_hash::fuzz_probe(&[u8]) -> bool as the deserializer fuzz entry point (never panics; drains typed reads under Lenient policy). Wire it into the W10 fuzzing campaign's target set. Landed with fm-6h6.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT ADDENDUM — division of labor vs the e2e harness (fm-fjq): this bead owns the VERIFICATION PLANES (oracles, self-goldens, Gallery, engine-equivalence, fuzzing) — the assertion machinery and corpora; fm-fjq owns the SCENARIO HARNESS that drives full-stack runs through real surfaces with log assertions and repro-bundling. Self-golden infrastructure is shared: e2e scenarios assert against goldens this bead's rig locks. Neither subsumes the other; both are G-gate load-bearing (goldens block always; e2e blocks from G2).

title: W10: The performance rig — PG-1..PG-8 + PG-A on pinned profiles, with scaling annex
description:
Implement §17.2's gate machinery: dedicated PINNED bare-metal profiles (8-core x86-64 Linux; Apple-silicon macOS), multiple repetitions, median + robust dispersion, ALERT vs BLOCKING thresholds, versioned baselines.

THE GATES: PG-1 end-to-end (OpeningManimExample-class 1080p vs the Python Reference under the PINNED benchmark definition from W1PROF — ≤0.5x at G2, ≤0.35x at G4); PG-2 rasterizer (≥300 Mpx/s fill-equivalent, ≥120 Mpx/s stroke at 8 threads on canonical workloads with stated path/segment/overdraw parameters); PG-3 throughput (≥60fps 1080p preview median primitive scene; ≥30fps 4K export typical 2D); PG-4 latency (cold CLI → first frame <150ms; edit-to-frame <1s); PG-5 determinism (bit-identical frames {1,4,16} per commit — frame-parallel and pipelined schedules INCLUDED — {32,96}+ weekly, certified matrix under --reproducible); PG-6 memory (≤1.5GB peak on the 4K 3D gallery under the RAM-derived in-flight budget; zero leaks over 1h soak; ZERO steady-state per-frame heap allocations on the primitive corpus); PG-7 typesetting (<3ms cold / <100µs cached median formula; 10k-glyph layout <20ms); PG-8 binding tax by class (from W10TAX's budgets).

SCALING PROFILES (published, then ratcheted): parallel efficiency at {1,8,32,96} threads for raster-bound and frame-parallel workloads, per release, alert-first graduating to blocking after two releases of baseline. PG-A: annex engines on their own pinned profiles (Apple Max-class; RTX-4090-class), gating ANNEX changes only — the CPU engine stands alone so acceleration can never mask a core regression (R21).

Rig telemetry beyond fps (§17.2): bytes up/back per frame, reuse and tile-cache hit rates, dirty-tile percentage, allocations per frame, team utilization, cross-NUMA steals, GPU occupancy, encode queue depth; scene classes static/locally-dirty/full-screen/dense-stroke/text-heavy/high-overdraw/3D; latency probes + 10-minute thermal runs; outputs raw/PNG/software-video/hardware-video.

Acceptance: every gate has a bench binary, committed baseline, variance budget, and flamegraph-on-regression artifact; blocking wired per gate's schedule (G2/G4 thresholds staged); the scaling annex publishes per release; a demonstrated regression drill (inject slowdown → gate blocks).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT NOTE — dependency correction: the rig does not depend on fm-zoi (binding-tax program); PG-8's class table activates when the Python bridge lands (G4a wiring), while PG-1/PG-2/PG-3/PG-5/PG-6/PG-7 gates run without any Python dependency. This keeps G2's PG enforcement off the fmn-python critical path, matching §20.3's gate criteria.

title: W11: Release binaries — per-SIMD-tier artifacts, selection UX, install script
description:
Package the engine (§17.3, §20.2 W11): release binaries per platform x SIMD build tier — portable / x86-64-v3 (AVX2) / x86-64-v4 (AVX-512) / aarch64+NEON — with install/launch tier SELECTION UX (the installer or first-run picks the right tier from HardwareTopology; a wrong-tier launch fails cleanly with guidance, never SIGILL); the curl|bash install script (scripts/install.sh — platform detect, tier detect, signed artifact fetch, checksum verify, PATH setup); signed releases with checksums; reproducible release builds (the release pipeline pins the toolchain per SUITE.lock and records provenance — the determinism doctrine applied to our own artifacts).

Size/time budgets (R19): embedded fonts + Studio UI assets are the size drivers — feature-gated where tiering allows, CI-MEASURED budgets on binary size and compile time with alert thresholds (a font-bundle or asset regression is visible, not silent).

Tier matrix discipline (R22): tiers capped at the four named; per-commit CI builds the two pinned profiles, the full matrix builds weekly; if CI budgets breach, a tier drops to weekly-only by recorded decision.

Acceptance: artifacts for all platform/tier combinations build in release CI; the install script tested on clean containers/VMs per platform (fresh-machine render works end-to-end with zero preinstalled deps — the leapfrog #1 demo, minus optional ffmpeg); tier-selection tests incl. the wrong-tier failure path; checksum/signature verification in the script; size/time budget reports in CI.

title: W11: Python wheels — CPython x platform ABI matrix, the manimlib namespace policy
description:
Package the Python front door (§20.2 W11, §15): wheels for the CPython x platform ABI matrix (abi3 where PyO3's support allows — decide and document the minimum CPython), bundling the engine + fonts so pip install franken-manim → from manimlib import * works with ZERO system dependencies (the one-binary story, wheel edition).

THE NAMESPACE POLICY (decide deliberately, document publicly): the bridge presents the manimlib module surface (§15.2) — resolve how the wheel provides it (a manimlib shim package? an import hook? a namespace decision recorded with its conflict story vs upstream manim installs in the same environment — a user with both installed must get a predictable, documented outcome, not import roulette).

Wheel contents: the compiled fmn-python extension per tier policy (portable tier for wheels, or per-arch wheels with tier selection — decide against R22's matrix budget); bundled OFL fonts with license texts; the fmd-math packs; NO ffmpeg (system ffmpeg discovered per D2's optional posture).

Acceptance: wheels build in release CI for the declared matrix; a clean-venv smoke test per platform (pip install → render the Hello corpus scene → bytes verified); the namespace-conflict test matrix (with/without upstream manim present); wheel-size budget recorded; the ABI/namespace policy documents published.

title: W11: npm/WASM packaging — the browser artifacts
description:
Package the WASM tiers (§20.2 W11, §10.7): the tier-1 frame renderer (W5WASM) as an npm package — ESM module + typed JS surface via wasm-bindgen, single-threaded default with the threads variant behind cross-origin-isolation documentation; the tier-2 serialized-timeline player as it lands at G5 (consuming W4TIMELINE's format; the browser Studio endgame's substrate).

Packaging discipline: artifact-size budgets CI-measured (R19 — fonts are the driver; the wasm bundle feature-gates faces and loads packs on demand where the design allows); version lockstep with the engine (the npm package version pins the engine commit; a serialized timeline records the engine version and the player refuses mismatches per §6.7's versioning policy); no CDN assumptions (the package is self-contained; consumers host it).

Acceptance: npm package builds + publishes in release CI (dry-run publish gate); a browser integration test (bundler + headless browser rendering a primitive scene from the package); size budget recorded; the version-mismatch refusal test; README/typing docs for the JS surface.

title: W11: Font & license bundles, embedded assets, cache/config conventions, release docs
description:
Finish distribution's obligations (§20.2 W11): the FONT + LICENSE BUNDLE — Computer Modern (roman/bold/italic/bold-italic), CM Typewriter, IBM Plex Sans, Noto Sans Math with their OFL license texts shipped in every artifact class (binary, wheel, npm), plus a font-bundle MANIFEST (names, versions, content hashes — the same hashes the input closure and typeset cache key against, §16.7: one identity everywhere); EMBEDDED STUDIO UI assets (compiled into the binary, versioned with it — no runtime file serving, §13.5); CACHE/CONFIG DIRECTORY CONVENTIONS (per-platform locations replacing appdirs/diskcache — documented, respected by --clear-cache and fmn doctor, stable across upgrades with a migration note policy); release DOCUMENTATION (install matrix, tier explanation, ffmpeg-optional posture, the Behavior Notes' distribution home, the certified-determinism user guide: what --reproducible promises and requires).

Licensing care: bundled font redistribution audited against each OFL text (attribution requirements met in the shipped manifest); the engine's own MIT+rider license text in every artifact; the CC BY-NC-SA corpus fixtures NEVER ship in any artifact (private fixtures stay private — §15.3's policy has a distribution-side enforcement check).

Acceptance: bundle manifest generated + hash-verified in CI across all artifact classes; license-completeness check (every bundled font's OFL text present; no unlicensed asset ships); config/cache convention tests per platform; the no-corpus-leak check in release CI; docs published with the release pipeline.

title: GATE G1 — Core 2D: the engine end-to-end without text
description:
Convergence gate G1 (plan §20.3) — an integration CHECKPOINT, not a scope reduction. Criteria, verbatim from the plan and mechanically checkable:

(1) Path-invariant + kernel fixtures green (W2's QuadPath, converter, arclength). (2) Core Mobject/VMobject functionality (W3's arena, RecordBuffer, family, ordering, updaters). (3) Transform family, partial reveals, fades, updaters, trackers running on the rational clock (W4). (4) The analytic renderer with G0-2's look-study calibration applied (W5 fill/strokes/adaptive-AA/certified engine). (5) THE PRIMITIVE CORPUS: ~25 scenes, OURS, public, bit-locked as self-goldens, THREAD-COUNT-INVARIANT ({1,4,16} per PG-5's per-commit sweep), and Look-Gallery-approved against Reference imagery. (6) Native PNG/y4m output (no ffmpeg needed to pass this gate).

The gate closes when every dependency bead is closed AND a gate-review session records: self-golden lock state, the Gallery verdict sheet for the primitive corpus, and the thread-sweep evidence. If the Gallery verdict includes any 'regression' entries, the gate stays open (R2: look regressions block the gate that introduced them).

title: GATE G2 — The Native Word: text and mathematics with zero external software (the flagship gate)
description:
Convergence gate G2 (plan §20.3) — the flagship: Text, MarkupText, and fmd-math tier-1 rendering with ZERO external software. Criteria:

(1) The harvest's tier-1 construct set (G0-4) lays out CORRECTLY — verified against TeX's published rules, not pixels — and BEAUTIFULLY (Look Gallery review vs LaTeX-rendered references: side-by-side indistinguishable-at-a-glance is the bar). (2) The span map drives isolate / t2c / substring slicing / TransformMatchingTex end-to-end. (3) The de-TeX'd classes (Brace, Matrix, DecimalNumber, marks) are native (W7DETEX). (4) SVGMobject works for user files (W2SVG). (5) Typeset caching live (W6TEX + W8CACHE). (6) The coverage-ratchet dashboard is PUBLIC and live (W6RATCHET). (7) fmd itself renders $...$ in HTML/PDF via the same crates — the cross-repo payoff demonstrated. (8) PERFORMANCE GATES ENFORCED: PG-1(G2) — ≤0.5x the Python Reference wall-clock — and PG-7 (typesetting latency) turn BLOCKING at this gate (W10PERF wiring).

Risk R1's escalation path binds here: if coverage misses the checkpoint, the gate is amended PUBLICLY with a construct-sprint plan; if quality misses, a calibration sprint blocks the gate. There is deliberately no LaTeX escape hatch to reach for — that is the design, not an oversight.

title: GATE G3 — Depth & Motion: 3D, interactivity, the Studio, the annex preview
description:
Convergence gate G3 (plan §20.3). Criteria:

(1) 3D surfaces with the KEPT lighting model; CameraFrame on the fixed Rotation conventions; depth, transparency, clip planes (W53D). (2) Dot clouds, images, textures (W53D/W73D + codecs). (3) Vector fields + StreamLines on the tuned integrator (W73D). (4) Events + InteractiveScene (W9EVENTS). (5) The Studio baseline: supervisor/worker, PNG stream, scrubbing, TUI (W9SUPER/W9STUDIO). (6) THE ANNEX SERVING STUDIO PREVIEW on supported Apple-silicon hardware (W5METAL) — with the DECLARED FALLBACK ruled in Rev 4 (OQ-12): if the annex misses, G3 passes on CPU preview meeting PG-3, and the annex requirement moves to G5 with a public note. Core gates stay CPU-only either way, so acceleration can never mask a core miss (R21). (7) PG-3 (throughput) blocking; PG-A active for annex changes.

Gate review records: the interactive-session demo evidence, Studio security-model review sign-off (§13.5's checklist), the annex-or-fallback decision with its public note if exercised.

title: GATE G4a — The Python Gallery: real scenes, source-unedited
description:
Convergence gate G4a (plan §20.3; sibling of G4b after G3). Criteria:

(1) VIDEO_CORPUS.lock FROZEN (W10CORPUS): the exact scene allowlist, helper commits, asset manifest. (2) The subclass bridge green (W10PY: G0-5's suite at full scale). (3) Import conformance vs the ACTUAL wildcard namespace (W10SCHEMA's harness). (4) Asset virtualization working (corpus assets resolve through the capability/cache layer). (5) THE CRITERION: the enumerated scenes run with SOURCE UNEDITED under the documented shims (imports, asset paths, fonts) and pass structural assertions (object counts, timings, bounding envelopes) plus Look-Gallery review. NOT pixel diffs — the Rev-3 contract (§4, §15.3). (6) TeX-pending scenes named with their missing constructs, feeding the public ratchet. (7) PG-8's binding-tax class table PUBLISHED with measured budgets (W10TAX).

R10's rule binds: any Python-semantics gap discovered at scale is tiered in the Parity Ledger BEFORE this gate closes — surprises become documented tiers, not silent breakage. R13: out-of-era scenes are excluded per-scene with recorded reasons.

title: GATE G4b — Certified Reproducibility: --reproducible end-to-end
description:
Convergence gate G4b (plan §20.3; sibling of G4a after G3; leapfrog #2). Criteria:

(1) The INPUT CLOSURE complete (§16.7): sources/modules, engine + SUITE.lock, toolchain + target features, config bytes, RNG seeds, asset/font hashes, execution-engine/backend identities + SIMD build tier, locale/timezone, capability policy — content-hashed into the sidecar provenance manifest. (2) fmn-dmath exclusively on certified paths (and the fixed-point raster boundary if G0-6 so decided). (3) Bundled-assets-only mode enforced (no AssetFetcher, bundled fonts only). (4) BIT-IDENTICAL raw frames + canonical PNGs + WAV across the certified matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64; Windows per OQ-6's separate decision) — including the deterministic parallel PNG-sequence bytes (W8CODEC2) and the deterministic sound mix (W8MIX). (5) Sidecar manifests emitted and verified. (6) PERFORMANCE GATES: PG-1(G4) — ≤0.35x the Python Reference — and PG-5 (the full determinism sweep including frame-parallel and pipelined schedules) blocking.

The closing property, stated once: the only uncertified artifact class left in the system is ffmpeg's — by construction, not by exception list. Gate review records the cross-platform hash evidence and a third-party-reproducible demo (repro bundle → fresh machine → identical bytes).

title: GATE G5 — Distribution & Leapfrogs: shipped, browsable, accelerated, extended
description:
Convergence gate G5 (plan §20.3). Criteria:

(1) W11 SHIPPED: per-tier binaries with selection UX, wheels with the namespace policy, npm/WASM, font/license bundles, reproducible releases (all four W11 beads). (2) The WASM timeline player (tier 2) and the browser Studio direction demonstrated (W11WASM building on W5WASM + W4TIMELINE). (3) Graph/DataFrame/Markdown mobjects landed at enhanced-tier quality (W7DATA lineage). (4) The fmd-math ratchet CONTINUING past its G2 floor into tier-2 constructs (the ratchet is a permanent public metric, not a gate-passing artifact). (5) The Accelerator Annex BROADENED: the CUDA production backend from its ledger spike (OQ-10's outcome), wider platform tuning per §17.6's playbooks, the autotune corpus grown. (6) The exploratory tier OPENED (differentiable-animation research, SME/AMX investigations — explicitly research, never product-gated).

This gate also inherits any deferred obligations with public notes: the annex-preview requirement if G3 exercised its fallback (OQ-12), Windows bit-certification if OQ-6 resolved positive, tier-2 construct sprints scheduled by corpus rank.

title: W1: Program governance — ADRs, workstream activation limits, gate ownership, the upstream ledger ritual (R9)
description:
Stand up the governance machinery that risk R9 (program bandwidth across eleven workstreams, High severity) names as its mitigation — governance is a deliverable, not a vibe:

(1) MAX SIMULTANEOUSLY-ACTIVE WORKSTREAMS: a declared cap (recorded in the governance doc; the plan's rule is that breaching governance HALTS NEW ACTIVATION — encode the check into the session workflow: before activating a workstream's first bead, count active workstreams). (2) GATE OWNERSHIP: each gate G0-G5 has a named owner responsible for the gate-review session and its recorded evidence (self-golden state, Gallery verdicts, thread-sweep proofs — the gate beads list what). (3) ADRs: an Architecture Decision Record convention (docs/adr/NNNN-*.md) — every amendment to the plan's decision log D-01..D-24 and every OQ resolution lands as an ADR with context/decision/consequences; the plan document is then trued up to match. (4) REVIEW RULES + REQUIRED COVERAGE BEFORE HANDOFF: a workstream may not hand off with failing gates or unwritten fixtures (AGENTS.md's landing-the-plane rules, made checkable). (5) STOP CONDITIONS + THE LEAPFROG-POSTPONEMENT POLICY: enhanced-tier/leapfrog work (W7DATA lineage, annex broadening, exploratory tier) is postponable BY POLICY and that postponement NEVER weakens core work — the policy text is the artifact. (6) THE UPSTREAM LEDGER RITUAL (§2.9): UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md tracks primitives that belong in foundation crates — seed entries: fmd-font factoring + outline decoder; fmd-math itself; fmd CFF outlines (tiered); fnx layout kernels (audit first); fsci Rotation-conventions exposure; fnp structured-record lerp fast path; ft CUDA device path — each with owner, status, and the cross-repo SUITE.lock coordination steps (R8, R17: upgrades deliberate, Gauntlet-diffed).

Acceptance: the governance doc + ADR template committed; UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md seeded with the seven entries above; the workstream-activation and handoff checklists referenced from AGENTS.md; first ADR filed (retroactively recording the Rev-4 contract pivot as ADR-0001 so the convention has a worked example).
close_reason: Governance machinery landed: docs/GOVERNANCE.md (activation cap 4 + check, gate ownership + evidence packets, ADR convention + true-up rule, checkable handoff, stop conditions + leapfrog-postponement policy, upstream ledger ritual); docs/adr/ with TEMPLATE + ADR-0001 (Rev-4 contract, retroactive), ADR-0002 (D-11 ratification record), ADR-0003 (dev/fuzz allowlist tiers — unblocks fm-ntp); UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md seeded with the seven §2.9 entries; AGENTS.md references the activation and handoff checklists.

title: W5: WASM tier 2 — the serialized-timeline player (implementation)
description:
Implement the tier-2 WASM deliverable (§10.7, §13.5's browser endgame substrate, G5 criterion): the SERIALIZED-TIMELINE PLAYER — a browser artifact that consumes W4TIMELINE's canonical serialized format (keyframes, labels, segment boundaries, engine version) and plays it: decode → reconstruct compiled render state → render frames on the tier-1 renderer (W5WASM) → present to canvas, with scrub/seek via the Timeline's seek semantics (pure segments reconstruct from snapshot + alpha; the serialized form carries the snapshots/checkpoints it needs — define exactly what a self-contained timeline bundle includes: geometry snapshots, typeset paths (pre-typeset — fmd-math need not run in the player for tier 2), styles, camera tracks, audio track reference).

Design decisions to make and record: (a) the TIMELINE BUNDLE format — self-contained (embedded assets/snapshots) vs referencing (player fetches) — lean self-contained per the sovereignty doctrine; (b) version policy per §6.7 (the player REFUSES engine-version mismatches cleanly — W11WASM's packaging test depends on this); (c) what tier 2 does NOT include, stated honestly: no scene construction, no Python, no live typesetting — it is a PLAYER (the browser Studio's live-render endgame remains W9STUDIO's WASM direction at G5, distinct from this bead).

Feeds: W11WASM packages this artifact; W9STUDIO's share/export story ("export an interactive player bundle of this scene") is the user-facing payoff.

Acceptance: a corpus scene exported as a timeline bundle plays in a headless browser test bit-consistently (same bundle ⇒ same frame hashes, single-threaded); scrub/seek correctness vs the native engine's frames on pure segments; version-mismatch refusal test; bundle-size measurement recorded (R19); the bundle format documented in the §6.7 versioned-schema registry.

title: W5: The CUDA annex production backend — via frankentorch, per the §17.6 playbook (post-ledger-spike)
description:
Implement the annex's second engine (§17.5, §17.6, §10.7, D-22; gated on OQ-10's ledger-spike outcome from G0-8): CUDA via frankentorch — ft is the ONLY GPU gateway; no wgpu, no direct CUDA bindings.

The §17.6 NVIDIA playbook, implemented: keep the scene RESIDENT — path headers, quadratic segments, styles, instances, image descriptors, macrotile bins, and fine-tile command lists live on-device (24GB is ample for persistent scene state + several 4K surfaces); upload ONLY DELTAS (changed transforms, styles, geometry ranges, ordering metadata — W5IR's revision taxonomy is exactly the delta vocabulary). Batch by pipeline class (solid fills, strokes, gradients, images, 3D triangles) WITHIN painter-order command runs; one block or cooperative block group per tile; raster and composite in stable command order; DETERMINISTIC count/prefix/scatter binning — never one-kernel-per-mobject, never unordered atomic appends. A small pinned-memory ring + multiple non-default streams overlap delta upload, rasterization, NV12 readback, and host-side ffmpeg submission; shared memory stages command/segment chunks ONLY where it provably removes repeated global reads (occupancy costs are real). Don't over-engineer the bus: 4K@30 readback is ~1GB/s RGBA / ~0.4GB/s NV12 against ~25GB/s PCIe gen4 x16 — plain pinned readback is fine; zero-copy is an Apple luxury. NVENC rides the §14.3 boundary. Tensor/RT cores are the WRONG tools here (branchy geometric coverage, ordered compositing, memory movement) — ordinary CUDA execution, good binning, persistent layout.

Hard boundaries (identical to Metal, R21): standard-only, never certified; PG-A profiles (RTX-4090-class pinned machine) gate ANNEX changes only; backend identity journaled; engine-equivalence budget vs certified frames; annex work pauses when core PG-1..PG-3 regress.

Acceptance: the corpus rendering through CUDA within the engine-equivalence budget; delta-upload instrumentation (bytes/frame recorded, resident-state verified across frames); binning determinism test (same scene ⇒ same command lists across runs); PG-A rig numbers on the pinned CUDA profile; graceful absence (no CUDA ⇒ CPU/other engines, no error).

title: W6: fmd-math tier 2 — the long-tail construct program, scheduled by corpus rank
description:
The post-G2 continuation of the coverage ratchet (§11.5, G5 criterion: "the ratchet continuing past its G2 floor into tier-2"): implement the LONG-TAIL constructs — \substack, uncommon decorations, exotic environments, and whatever else G0-4's harvest ranked below the tier-1 cut — in DESCENDING OCCURRENCE ORDER (the harvest's ranked table is the schedule; re-rank as the corpus-pending list from W10CORPUS adds real-scene evidence).

Working rules inherited from tier 1: each construct lands with (a) layout per the published TeX rules (Appendix-G parameters where applicable) over the calibrated metrics; (b) span-map provenance (W6SPAN's invariant: every glyph/rule carries source spans); (c) Gallery review against LaTeX renders for visually-nontrivial constructs; (d) the ratchet baseline UPDATED in the same change (coverage strictly increases — W6RATCHET's monotone rule); (e) its precise unsupported-error REMOVED and the error-audit inventory updated. Until a construct lands, its named error and tracking link remain the contract (never silence, never garbage).

Corpus-pending discipline (§15.3): scenes in VIDEO_CORPUS.lock marked pending-with-named-constructs get UNBLOCKED as their constructs land — each tier-2 construct's completion note lists which corpus scenes it unblocks, and the G4a harness re-runs them (the ratchet's real-world half advancing in lockstep).

This bead is deliberately open-ended: treat it as the tier-2 EPIC-of-record — file per-construct child beads as they are scheduled, each with the (a)-(e) checklist, so `br` reflects the actual sprint plan R1's escalation path calls for ("gate amended publicly with a construct-sprint plan" if G2 coverage misses).

Acceptance (per increment): construct green under (a)-(e); ratchet dashboard reflects the increase; unblocked corpus scenes pass. Bead closes only when the harvest's tier-2 list is exhausted or remaining constructs are formally moved to the out-of-tier ledger with revisit triggers (§16.6).

title: W7: Code + MarkdownMobject — fmd's highlighter and document engine as animatable content
description:
Implement the document-flavored mobjects (§11.7, Appendix A census; leapfrog #10):

CODE: the census class (text_mobject module) binding FMD'S SYNTAX HIGHLIGHTER (the pygments displacement — §1.7, §2.3: the highlighter ships in fmd and displaces pygments verbatim) over CM TYPEWRITER — language selection, style/theme mapping (owned themes; document which pygments style names map where), line numbers per the Reference's constructor surface, and the StringMobject indexing conventions so Code[…] slicing works like Text (via W6SPAN's provenance — the highlighter's token spans ARE source spans; wire them through rather than re-deriving).

MARKDOWNMOBJECT (enhanced tier): fmd's parser + Scribe layout + fmd-math INLINE mathematics ($...$ in a document renders through the same engine as Tex — the §11.7 promise: documents and slides as animatable content, the same renderer that powers fmd's own HTML/PDF math). Scope tier-1 of the mobject honestly: block structure (headings, paragraphs, lists, code fences via Code, block math), inline emphasis/code/math; tables and images as the documented next increments. Each element maps to a submobject subtree with source-span provenance so TransformMatchingStrings works ACROSS document edits — the killer demo (edit a slide, animate the diff).

Tier discipline: enhanced-tier — never blocks core gates; lands after W6TEXT/W6TEX at leisure, meets the same bars (fixtures, Ledger rows, self-goldens, unit tests + e2e scenes with logging).

Acceptance: Code fixture corpus (multi-language, slicing, line numbers, theme application) with span-map tests; MarkdownMobject structural fixtures per element class; an e2e demo scene (slide edit animated via matching) in the feature corpus with a self-golden; inline-math parity test (same string in MarkdownMobject and Tex ⇒ identical glyph paths); Ledger rows.

title: W7: NeuralNetworkMobject — real ft-nn modules as animatable structure (exploratory boundary drawn)
description:
Implement the frankentorch content role (§2.6, leapfrog #9): NeuralNetworkMobject renders REAL ft-nn modules — layer/graph structure introspected from an actual ft module (not a drawing that looks like a network), live weight/activation visualization mid-training (values flow from ft tensors into mobject styles via updaters — the ValueTracker pattern generalized to tensor sources), and animation of forward/backward passes (activation wavefronts, gradient-flow highlights) built on the standard animation machinery.

Design care: ft tensor access happens at DEFINED points in the frame loop (an updater reading a training loop's tensors is a stateful effect — register the effect signature with W4PURITY's classifier: NN scenes classify stateful, correctly); large-layer rendering leans on W5IR's instancing (a 4096-unit layer is one compiled dot + 4096 instances); determinism note — a scene visualizing live training is only as deterministic as the training loop (document: seeded ft runs reproduce; unseeded runs are standard-mode content).

THE EXPLORATORY BOUNDARY, drawn explicitly (§2.6, G5's exploratory tier): DIFFERENTIABLE ANIMATION — autograd through animation parameters toward a differentiable-renderer surrogate — is RESEARCH, its own exploratory program, never product-gated and NOT part of this bead; this bead's deliverable is the content mobject only. SME/AMX and Neural Engine acceleration remain exploratory-tier per §17.6 (no safe Rust path; wrong shape for the workload).

Tier discipline: enhanced/backlog — never blocks core gates.

Acceptance: structure introspection fixtures over representative ft-nn modules (MLP, conv stack); live-value updater test against a scripted training loop (values observed at defined frames, logged); purity-classification test (NN scenes demote to stateful); an e2e demo scene with self-golden (seeded run); Ledger row marked enhanced-tier.

title: W10: The Rust front door — prelude, fluent surface, builder ergonomics, examples (§15.1 owned)
description:
Own the Rust API as a first-class deliverable (§15.1, D-11's second half) — its pieces exist across G0-1 (the ratified prototype), W3 (arena + builders), and W10SCHEMA (generated config structs), but the USER-FACING SURFACE needs an owner:

Scope: `fmn::prelude` — the curated import surface mirroring `from manimlib import *`'s ergonomics (constants, directions, colors, mobject types, animations, the stage/scene entry points) without glob-import chaos (documented, stable, schema-checked); the FLUENT SURFACE exactly as G0-1 proved it (scoped stage context; deferred-command .animate recording mirroring the builder semantics; snake_case throughout; kwargs displaced by Default config structs + builder methods GENERATED from the schema — hand-written builder drift is a build error via W10SCHEMA); Result/error ergonomics (fmn::Result, error taxonomy: capability errors, typesetting errors with construct names, config diagnostics — each matching the CLI's exit-code table); EXAMPLES as compiled artifacts — the primitive corpus scenes are the Rust API's living documentation, plus doc-tested snippets per major type (cargo test runs them; the README's Rust example must actually compile — keep it in sync).

Why owned: "a first-class Rust API proven by a compiling prototype" is design commitment #5 (§0) — if it stays distributed across beads, the ergonomic whole (what a new Rust user actually types) never gets reviewed as a product. The G1 primitive corpus is written against THIS surface.

Acceptance: prelude + fluent surface documented and schema-checked; all primitive-corpus scenes compile against the public API only (no crate-internal imports); doc-tests green in CI; error-taxonomy tests (each error class constructed and matched); an API-review session recorded (the ergonomics pass: naming, builder chains, discoverability) with resulting ADR if the G0-1 shape is amended.

title: W10: The Gauntlet e2e harness — scripted end-to-end scenarios with detailed structured logging
description:
Build the END-TO-END test harness that every epic's beads register scenarios with — the program-wide answer to "how do we KNOW it all works together after implementation":

THE HARNESS: scripted e2e scenarios driven through the REAL surfaces (the fmn CLI, the Rust API, fmn-python, the Studio's HTTP surface, the batch runner) — never through test-only backdoors — each scenario a checked-in script/manifest declaring: the scene/inputs, the invocation (flags, config, determinism mode, engine), the ASSERTIONS (bit-locked hashes via the self-golden rig for certified paths; structural assertions — object counts, timings, envelopes — for standard paths; exit codes; emitted-file inventory; NDJSON schema validation for robot outputs), and the LOG EXPECTATIONS (see below). Scenario classes to seed: render-matrix (formats x quality x reproducible x engine, vs fake-ffmpeg and native sinks); determinism drills (same scene at {1,4,16} threads and twice-in-a-row ⇒ identical bytes, wired into PG-5); failure-path drills (ffmpeg absent ⇒ named capability error; unsupported TeX construct ⇒ precise error; corrupt cache ⇒ recovery; worker crash ⇒ supervisor restart); lifecycle drills (edit→rebuild→restore→replay; checkpoint_paste; scrub); Python-vs-Rust parity drills (the same scene through both front doors ⇒ identical structural results).

DETAILED LOGGING AS A TESTED CONTRACT (§17.1, §18): every e2e run captures the owned structured-tracing output (scene→play→frame→phase→tile spans, stage timings, counters, decision points: engine selected, ExecutionPlan derived, cache hits, purity classifications, barriers) into a per-run artifact; scenarios ASSERT ON LOGS where behavior is otherwise invisible (e.g. "the preflight typeset N strings before frame 0", "the wait() frames hit the tile cache", "the segment classified pure") — so a regression in the machinery, not just the pixels, fails the run; on ANY failure the harness auto-bundles the repro (scene + input closure + logs + journal tail per §18's repro-bundle machinery) so every red e2e run is a one-command deterministic replay.

DOCTRINE (binding, cross-referenced from every epic): each feature bead registers at least one e2e scenario when it lands user-visible behavior; unit tests live in-crate with the bead; the harness runs the fast tier per-commit and the full matrix nightly (CI budgets per R22).

Acceptance: the harness + scenario format + log-assertion machinery running in CI with the seed scenario classes above; the repro-bundle-on-failure path demonstrated; per-epic registration documented in AGENTS.md's testing section; a deliberately-injected regression drill per scenario class (break it → red with useful logs → repro bundle works).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: AUDIT NOTE — dependency correction: the harness does NOT depend on the finished CLI (fm-c53); it grows scenario classes as surfaces land (Rust API first, CLI/Studio/Python scenarios registered by those beads when they arrive). This keeps the harness off G2's critical path while G2 still requires the harness itself (log assertions + repro bundling operational).

title: W2: Migrate fmn-geom smoothing solves onto fsci-linalg
description: fm-e3f landed the anchor-mode smoothing (crates/fmn-geom/src/smoothing.rs) with temporary OWNED banded/dense Gaussian-elimination solvers, because the FrankenSuite dependency plumbing (SUITE.lock, fm-g2c) was not yet in place. Doctrine D4 routes banded + dense solves through fsci-linalg. Once fm-g2c makes frankenscipy consumable, replace solve_banded/solve_dense/band_to_dense in smoothing.rs with fsci-linalg calls, keep the public smooth_cubic_handles/smooth_quadratic_path signatures, and confirm the smooth_cubic_handles.txt scipy-derived fixtures stay green at 1e-9.

title: W2: Sweep fmn-geom transcendental shim onto fmn-dmath
description: fmn-geom funnels every sin/cos/acos/atan2 call through crates/fmn-geom/src/scalar.rs, currently delegating to std (platform libm — fine for standard mode, not certified). When fm-7y6 lands the fmn-dmath deterministic elementary-function layer, repoint scalar.rs at it (one-file change by design) and delete the shim if fmn-dmath's API makes it redundant [do NOT delete the file without express permission — fold it instead]. sqrt stays std (IEEE-exact). Fixture tolerances in fmn-geom tests may tighten afterward.
close_reason: Swept: fmn-geom/src/scalar.rs now delegates sin/cos/acos/atan2 to fmn-dmath (certified layer, always — geometry is object-space semantics; the FAST seam is renderer-side by design). Verified no stray std transcendentals remain in fmn-geom src; all 43 fmn-geom tests green unchanged (Reference-parity tolerances comfortably absorb the ≤1-ulp libm deltas); shim kept as the single funnel file rather than deleted.

title: W3: fnp-backed zero-copy NumPy round-trip for RecordBuffer
description: fm-cus landed the data plane with byte-layout tests locking the interleaved schema to numpy's structured-dtype packing (crates/fmn-mobject/tests/data_plane.rs), but the LIVE zero-copy round-trip through franken_numpy's structured-record engine (mobject.data as an fnp structured view over the RecordBuffer's current storage generation) needs fnp consumable, which arrives with SUITE.lock (fm-g2c). When it lands: export a RecordView-backed fnp structured array, assert zero-copy aliasing (writes both ways), the V3 detach on resize, and dtype equality with the Reference dtypes. The real PyO3/NumPy surface stays with fm-aqv.

title: W1: Swap fmn-core RNG internals onto fnp-random once SUITE.lock lands
description: fm-m1u implemented PCG64DXSM + SeedSequence OWNED in fmn-core/src/rng.rs (doctrine D-4 routes the RNG to franken_numpy's fnp-random, which is already bit-exact to NumPy, but fnp is not consumable until fm-g2c lands SUITE.lock). When it is: replace the generator/seeding internals with fnp-random calls, keep the RngRoot/Substream/fork_frame API and the canonical name encoding verbatim, and keep fixtures/rng_vectors.txt green unchanged (it pins NumPy bit-parity either way). Note for implementer: NumPy's PCG64DXSM seeds via the FULL 128-bit multiplier srandom then runs the cheap-multiplier DXSM step — the vectors catch any regression here.

title: Execute the one-time Reference imagery capture on a GL-capable machine
description: Run scripts/capture_reference_imagery.py (landed with fm-xb3) on a machine with the Reference's full GL import closure (moderngl + OpenGL context or EGL/OSMesa headless + manimpango). The harness renders the six-scene G0-2 calibration set (gradient fills, self-intersecting star, all joint types, glow, 3D lighting, Pango text) and writes gallery/reference_captures/*.png + PROVENANCE.json (python/GL/package identity, Reference commit, per-image sha256). Then update the two tables in docs/look_gallery/CAPTURE_INVENTORY.md (statuses to 'captured', capture machine + date) and commit — the PNG bytes stay out of git per the §15.3 fixture policy. Capture ONCE; the environment is recorded, never maintained (D-16). Blocks fm-k77 (the G0-2 look study needs the captured imagery). Note: the scene definitions may need small adjustments against the real Reference API at capture time — they are written but could not be executed in the dev environment (no GL).

title: Migrate fmn-conformance .npy interchange onto fnp-io
description: crates/fmn-conformance/src/npy.rs is an owned, strict .npy v1.0 reader/writer (C-order <f8/<f4/<i8, capped headers/dims, byte-compatible with np.save) landed with fm-xb3 because no FrankenSuite crate is consumable yet (SUITE.lock pins repos; zero external deps wired). Once fnp enters the workspace via the R17 ritual (see fm-ai1/fm-go7/fm-n1b for the same pattern), swap the internals onto fnp-io while keeping the module's small public surface (NpyArray/NpyData/read_npy/write_npy/to_points) so tests do not change. The byte-compat lock in tests/npy_interchange.rs (write_npy(read_npy(bytes)) == bytes) must keep passing.

title: Record a real aarch64 topology snapshot fixture (needs hardware)
description: fm-x68 committed the real linux-x86_64 topology snapshot (crates/fmn-platform/fixtures/topology_linux-x86_64.snapshot.txt, 128-logical/64-core/8-CCD machine) and a synthetic aarch64 big.LITTLE sysfs tree fixture exercising the capacity-based P/E derivation. Still wanted per the acceptance: a snapshot from a REAL aarch64 machine (linux-aarch64 CI runner or Apple silicon under Linux/macOS fallback). Run: REGEN_TOPOLOGY=1 cargo test -p fmn-platform --test topology, then commit fixtures/topology_<os>-aarch64.snapshot.txt.

title: W4: dependency-gated animation classes — geometry-backed indication, numbers, string transforms
description:
The tail of the 80-class animation catalog (fm-cye) whose Reference constructors need mobject-library geometry, composition operators, or W6 span maps — every underlying MECHANISM already exists in fmn-anim; these are constructors + wiring only.

Geometry-backed indication (needs fm-oab: Dot/Circle/Line/SurroundingRectangle/Underline): FocusOn (Transform between Dots with an updater-tracked target), CircleIndicate, Flash (lines + ShowCreationThenDestruction per line, grouped), FlashAround/FlashUnder (VShowPassingFlash over a surrounding path), AnimationOnSurroundingRectangle + ShowPassingFlashAround/ShowCreationThenDestructionAround/ShowCreationThenFadeAround, Broadcast (specialized.py: LaggedStart of Restores over styled circles).

Composition-backed (needs fm-hfe AnimationGroup/Succession/LaggedStart): the grouped forms of Flash/Broadcast/AnimationOnSurroundingRectangle above, ShowCreationThenFadeOut (Succession), FlashyFadeIn (AnimationGroup), and wrapping transform_matching_parts' returned Vec into the real TransformMatchingParts group (run_time=2, lag_ratio=0) with clean_up_from_scene semantics (scene removes pieces, adds target — coordinate with fm-5xm).

Numbers (needs fm-y69 DecimalNumber): ChangingDecimal, ChangeDecimalToValue, CountInFrom — UpdateFromAlphaFunc-shaped over set_value.

Strings (needs fm-70s span maps): AddTextWordByWord (build_groups word boundaries), TransformMatchingStrings/TransformMatchingTex (matching_blocks over symbol substrings; the fsci assignment matcher enters at string_mobject's shape matching per D-09 — NOT in transform_matching_parts, confirmed against the pinned tree).

Acceptance: each class constructible with Reference-default parameters, doc-mapped to its reference file:line, effect signatures declared, tests per family.

title: W8: cargo-fuzz harnesses for fmn-codec parsers (inflate/png/jpeg)
description: fm-17m shipped the fmn-codec parsers with deterministic in-tree hostile-input sweeps (truncation, byte soup, bit-flip mutation, bomb budgets) asserting the §16.5 posture. The remaining R14 item is continuous coverage-guided fuzzing: cargo-fuzz targets for inflate_bytes, decode_png, and decode_jpeg with resource-budget assertions (error within budget, never hang, never overallocate). Blocker to resolve first: libfuzzer-sys/cargo-fuzz are dev-only dependencies outside the governed closure (D1) — the fuzz/ crate is non-authoritative tooling, but the allowlist ruling should be explicit (W1 governance, fm-1o8 territory). Wire into CI as a scheduled job, seed corpora from crates/fmn-codec/tests/fixtures.

title: W8: SVG export (vmobject_to_svg) — gated on the W2 SVG document processor
description: The fm-65l tail: vmobject_to_svg — trivial by design since paths are native quads (emit path data + styles), with documented fidelity limits (gradients per our model, no filter effects). Split out because the acceptance criterion is a round-trip through our own SVG importer (export → import → structural equality within tolerance), and the W2 hardened SVG document processor has not landed. Implement in fmn-codec (or alongside the importer) once the W2 bead ships; wire the round-trip test then.

title: Gate drift: crate-DAG check red on main (g0-3 spike naming; frame→dmath, scene→hash edges untracked)
close_reason: Pre-existing gate drift on main, found by the first check.sh run this session: (1) the G0-3 spike crate was named g0-3-fmd-math, missing the sanctioned fmn-spike-* exemption convention (g0-1 precedent) — renamed to fmn-spike-fmd-math (Cargo.toml + its tests/example imports + SUITE_ALLOWLIST.tsv row + Cargo.lock); (2) two real, downward, bead-sanctioned edges never trued into scripts/check_crate_dag.py EXPECTED: fmn-frame→fmn-dmath (D-17 deterministic transfer functions, fm-a25) and fmn-scene→fmn-hash (journal serialization+digests §13.4, fm-y7u). No plan amendment needed — §19 lists crates+layering, both edges point downward. crate-DAG check green (22 crates), governed-closure 6/6, full workspace 568 tests green.

title: W8: flaky test — fmn-output boundary::sandbox_publishes_atomically_and_pins_the_environment fails intermittently under full-suite parallelism, passes in isolation
notes: Second racy test observed 2026-07-24: sandbox_timeout_kills_and_leaves_destination_untouched fails intermittently under load (assertion matches!(result, Err(JobTimedOut)) at boundary.rs:617 — the scripted job completes before the timeout fires). Both flakes are timing-sensitive sandbox tests; reproduce by looping 'cargo test -p fmn-output --test boundary' under parallel build load. Roughly 1-in-3 under contention, passes in isolation.
